description:
Mako Naval is a game around the naval battle theme. Each player must cross the ocean with his ship and reach the other side before the others ships, while sinking and delaying the others ships thanks to mines.

On his turn, the player moves his boat forward one space in the direction of his choice and then rolls the die. The die indicates how far he can land a mine in any direction he chooses.

If the mine hits another boat, it returns to the starting point. If not, the mine stays and block the boat from others colors.
A boat can pass over a square with a mine of its own color and recover it.

The first boat to arrive on the other side of the board, facing its starting point, wins.

This game is taking place in the universe of the "Wacky Races" cartoon. The goal is to drive first one of your car in the right colored space at the opposite of the board. It's a game without any chance, only strategy.

When a car has to move, the player choose one of the three spaces in front of him. It can't go back or on a side space. All cars, from the beginning to the end of the games should be on spaces that touch either at an angle or on a side another car.

Galette complète is the ninth expansion for Blanc-Manger Coco. It contains 40 question cards and 160 answer cards themed about Brittany.

One player is designated "question master" and reads a blue card with a hole-in-the-wall sentence (s), all other players pose a white card. Whoever posed the funniest card wins the round then reads the next blue card and so on. The first player with 5 points is the lucky winner!

Based on the hit BBC Radio 4 panel game, the Unbelievable Truth Game is a game in which the truth is stranger than fiction. If you can tell fibs without batting an eyelid or you can embroider the truth like a pro, then this is the game for you.

Players take turns trying to smuggle unbelievable truths past their opponents in a series of quick fire and lecture rounds. If you can spot when a "truth" is fact or fiction, ring the bell to win a point.

boardgame: レディファースト (Lady First)
description:
レディファースト (Lady First) is a shedding game from Japan, with each card controlling which successive cards may be played.

The game has a romantic theme, with one set of red cards themed for a woman, and a blue set for a man. Each cards carries various romantic lines (all in Japanese), that the players ought to speak as they play, such as

"Don't let me go"
"I'm glad to see you"

However, the game is designed for any adult players, not simply opposites.

The red deck has 12 cards numbered from 1 to 12 (and this player starts), the blue deck has 11 cards from 2 to 12. If the blue player can get the red player to play all 12 of their cards first, blue wins. Otherwise, blue loses.

On their turn, a player may draw one card from their deck, up to a hand limit of five, or play a card to the table.

Along the bottom of each card is a set of numbers, one for blue and one for red. These say what card can be played after this card. For example, the blue 5 can be followed by a blue 2,3,4 and 6 or by a red 4 or red 6-12.

As players go through their deck, they must carefully decide which cards to hold and which to play, in order to push more cards out of their opponent's hand.
user summary
publishers: 米光と抜群のゲームデザイナーズ (Yonemitsu to batsugun no gēmudezaināzu)

boardgame: レディファースト (Lady First)
description:
レディファースト (Lady First) is a re-implementing of レディファースト (Lady First), with additional sets of cards to allow 3 or 4 players as well as the original 2 player game. Two sets of male cards have been added, otherwise the gameplay is the same.

The game has a romantic theme, with one set of red cards themed for a woman, and blue sets for a man. Each cards carries various romantic lines (all in Japanese), that the players ought to speak as they play.

Players draw and play out hand cards, with a set of numbers on each card controlling which others may follow it. If the blue player can get the red player to exhaust their cards first, blue wins. Otherwise, blue loses.

The red deck has 12 cards numbered from 1 to 12 (and this player starts), the blue deck has 11 cards from 2 to 12. On their turn, a player may draw one card from their deck, up to a hand limit of five, or play a card to the table.

Along the bottom of each card is a set of numbers, one for blue and one for red. These say what card can be played after this card. For example, the blue 5 can be followed by a blue 2,3,4 and 6 or by a red 4 or red 6-12.

As players go through their deck, they must carefully decide which cards to hold and which to play, in order to push more cards out of their opponent's hand.
user summary

In Treasure Cats you play as an anthropomorphic cat trying to find treasure. Each round you will choose to go to the one of two Locations. The Junkyard or the Farmstead,to find Items. Each Item has a Value. At the end of each round, players reveal their Treasure Cards and see which Location yielded the highest  Items value. All players who chose that Location get a Victory Point.

Objective:The first player to score 5 Victory Points wins if no other player has 5 or more Victory Points. 6 Victory Points wins if no other player has 6 or more Victory Points.7 Victory Points everyone with 7 shares the win.

Setup:Pick a starting player. At the beginning of each round, the starting player shuffles the cards and deals 2 cards face-down to each player. Then they deal 2 of the remaining cards face up in the center of the play area. Each round has 3 phases.

Phase 1: Choose Treasure Cards. 
Opening with the Start Player and moving clockwise, each player will choose a card in their hand as a Treasure Card. They will place this card face up in front of them. Using the other card, hide either the top or bottom half of the shown Treasure Card so that only the Item or Location is visible. *This is very important!

Phase 2: Trigger Active Abilities                                                                      
Beginning with the Start Player and continuing clockwise, each player displaying an Item with an Active (A) ability may use it.
For example, if a player is showing the Compass, they would choose another player with a revealed Item. The targeted player would have to discard their revealed Item card if they correctly guessed the targeted players location. 
Note: A player can only be targeted by the Compass, Abacus, Whistle and Dog Mask once per round.

Phase 3: Award Victory Points                                                                               
All players now reveal their chosen Treasure Cards unless their Treasure Card was discarded in Phase 2. Each player with a Treasure Card showing a Passive (P) ability may now use that ability.
Add up the value for each Item at each Location. The Location with the highest value of Items wins the round.
Each player with a revealed Treasure Card with the winning Location receives 1 Victory Point. If there is no winning Location, then all players with a revealed Treasure Card earn 1 Victory Point. If a player had to discard their Treasure Card, then they cannot receive Victory Points.
After Phase 3 is complete, check to see if any player has achieved the winning objective. If not, then pass the Start Player token clockwise and start a new round.

-description from publisher

The game was created by a Tunisian travel start-up to fight the pandemic.

The players start with the same amount of money and travel the world, rolling dice. 
When they arrive in a new country they have to pay for their stay (based on the average cost for each country), then they can continue travel, or decide to work there.
They just don't have enough cash to go around without stopping by and working in some of these locations (answering trivia questions).
They can also play special cards (Cruella) to delay their opponents.

The winner is the the first player to complete the World tour.
The goal of the game is also to teach tricks to players for their future travels.

There is an expansion for kids (optional but not sold separately !)

boardgame: Grimdark Future: Jakob's Path – Narrative Campaign
description:
Jakob’s Path is a narrative campaign for Grimdark Future that follows the story of Jakob, a ruthless Battle Brothers captain, in his quest to conquer the Orc world of Zeloria-M5, whilst searching for a mysterious artifact.

This game expansion for Grimdark Future takes players on an adventure across 10 missions which are tied together by an exciting story of epic proportions. Rules for unit progression are also included, so you can watch your armies grow and evolve throughout the campaign.

Even though this narrative campaign was written with Battle Brothers and Orc Marauders in mind, it is easy to imagine this type of clash to be happening between any of the other factions in the galaxy, so feel free to swap them out with whatever armies you have available to play.

Note that to play this campaign you’re going to need the core rules for Grimdark Future as well as some army lists

From the questionable to the downright detestable, it's time to find out what your friends really think of you with this game of moral dilemmas!
From "which of us is most likely to complain in a restaurant?" to "which of us is most likely to go to jail for tax evasion?", take turns to read out the cards and nominate a player.  It's the perfect party game for bad people!

—description from the back of the box

Join in on an adventure in the forest, where you will meet a lot of funny and cute animals.

The Animal Forest is a fun game for children that will take you no time to learn. However, no game played will be the same, as the game evolves differently every time you play.
Every player will get a plate, which consists of several pictures of animals, which need to be collected, in order to win the game. You should beware of the Big Bad Wolf, as collecting him will make you unable to win the game!

This game combines picture lottery with trading, stealing and collecting a lot of animals.

A football (soccer) trivia game for the whole family, with almost 3,000 questions included. The questions are divided into four categories, but the fifth category is the penalty box, where players can get into various kinds of trouble - or score straight into the goal! Three levels of difficulty, so players of all ages and experience can compete on level ground. Be the first to complete all the questions and get to the finish line!

—user summary

boardgame: Now That's What I Call Music: The Board Game
description:
From New Romantics and Britpop to house and rap, this is the soundtrack to our lives. Re-live the last few decades of great music in this fast-moving board game based on the chart-topping NOW THAT'S WHAT I CALL MUSIC albums.

—description from back of box

boardgame: The Best of Christmas Game: Not For Kids
description:
OK, so we admit we're not famous for naughty games. Clean-cut family games - that's us. But occasionally a silly, hilarious and ever so slightly crude idea occurs to us that needs an outlet and here it is: Best of Christmas NOT FOR KIDS. It's a bit of light-hearted grown-up trivia that's just a smidgeon too naughty for the younger kids, but still full of the laugh out loud fun that makes the LOGO family a British favourite!

From office party antics and their aftermath to what time we first reach for the bottle on Christmas Day, Best of Christmas NOT FOR KIDS covers the messy, boozy, mistletoe-clad reality of Christmas for adults - and from our research, most of us are making the naughty list.

—description from back of box

Third pack of six short Advanced Squad Leader tournament scenarios. These were designed for the 2020 West Coast Rumble.

Q13 – One Last Attempt – Dom Bütgenbach, Belgium, 22 December 1944: Game Length: 5½ Turns; Board q;  Americans: 5½ Squads, 2x Leaders, 2x Machineguns, 3x 60mm mortars, & Bazooka; vs. Germans: 6x SS-Squads, 3x Leaders, 2x Machineguns, & JdgPz IV/70; Estimated Playing Time: 2 hours 21 minutes

Q14 – True to Form – The Factory, Carroceto, Italy, 25 January 1944: Game Length: 5½ Turns; Board 71; Germans: 8x Squads, 4x Leaders, 2x Machineguns, FT, 2x DC, & 2x StuG 75/34(i); vs. British: 5x Squads, 2x Leaders, 3x Machineguns, & PIAT;  Estimated Playing Time 2 hours 38 minutes

Q15 – Mantes Meet Up – Mantes-Gassicourt, France, 27 August 1944: Game Length: 6 Turns; Board 10z; Americans: 11x Squads, 4x Leaders, 6x Machineguns, 2x Bazooka, & 3x M5A1; vs. Germans: 11x Squads, 3x Leaders, 4x Machineguns, & 2x StuG IIIG; Estimated Playing Time: 3 hours 26 minutes

Q16 – Defence of St. Oedenrode – St. Oedenrode, Holland, 17 September 1944: Game Length: 6 Turns; Board 59; Americans: 5x Squads, 2x Leaders, 3x Machineguns, 60mm Mortar, & Bazooka; vs. Germans: 10x Squads, 3x Leaders, & 3x Machineguns; Estimated Playing Time: 2 hours 31 minutes

Q17 – Absorbing the Thrust – Furnes, France, 28 May 1940: Game Length: 5½ Turns; Board 10z; French: 8x Squads, 3x Leaders, Machinegun, & 3x H35; vs. Germans: 6x Squads, Gun Crew 2x Leaders, Machinegun, 50mm Mortar, ATR, & 2x 3.7cm Anti-Tank Gun; Estimated Playing Time: 2 hours 50 minutes

Q18 – Seize the Crossroads – Briqueville, France, 29 June 1944: Game Length: 5½ Turns; Board 17; Germans: 5x Squads, Gun Crew, 2x Leaders, 2x Machineguns, 75mm Infantry Gun, & Fortifications;  vs. Americans: 12x Squads, HS, 3x Leaders, 2x Machineguns, 2x 60mm Mortars, 3x Bazookas, FT, & 3x DC; Estimated Playing Time: 2 hours 32 minutes

Each scenario is played on half a map board with a handful of counters per each side; these scenarios are fast, with a limited amount of SSR, to ensure minimum set up time and maximum game time.

-description from publisher's website

Never venture in the same old dungeon again.

Twisted Dungeons is a supplement for Four Against Darkness, suited for all levels of play, that will change your dungeons forever.

It’s easy to use: just roll d88 (two eight-sided dice) as the adventure starts, and read the corresponding numbered entry. The book will inject unpredictable events and themes into your dungeon exploration: 64 dungeon complications, ranging from mosquito infestation to teleporting mirrors, from slippery tunnels carved out of ice to chambers with magic-enhancing properties. Most complications will come with interesting rewards, in experience or loot.

Twisted Dungeons may be used alongside its companion volume, Twisted Minions, to create even more random variations. With a bit of common sense, it will work with any book in the Four Against Darkness line.

One of Mattel's All-Together games, with no loose pieces.  From the instructions:
"Be the first player to collect one of each of the four shapes before the SHAPE CHOMPER can gobble any of them up!"

boardgame: The Lost Clans (fan expansion for Age of War)
description:
Lost Clans introduces 5 new clans to the game, with a total of 12 new cards.  These clans add more difficult combinations and allow for a better experience with 4+ players.  The clans are as follows:

Utsunomiya - 1 card (4 pts) - 4 flipped
Saito - 2 cards (3 pts each) - 6 flipped
Otomo - 3 cards (2 pts, 3 pts, 4 pts) - 11 flipped
Kuroda - 3 cards (2 pts, 2 pts, 3 pts) - 9 flipped
Anegakoji - 3 cards (2 pts, 2 pts, 2 pts) - 7 flipped

All clans use real Japanese family names and crests, and have been designed to match the original style.
I ordered my cards from https://www.printplaygames.com/
 - Custom Size Rectangular Cards #1
 - Matte Premium Black Core
 - length and width 2.75 inches

Front and back templates are attached.

Sing, dance, and hug... then play this Trolls World Tour edition of UNO!

Includes an exclusive "Tiny Diamond's Mix Tape Rule," which forces the next player to draw a card from each player's hand.

And don't forget to yell "UNO!" before the party is over!
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

boardgame: The Offensive Band Name Generator: Album Cover Expansion
description:
This is an expansion for the party game The Offensive Band Name Generator. It includes 50 Album Cover cards, 2 brand new Theme Lists and a rules sheet.

With this expansion, players will draw album cover cards and work to make band names and album names that best fit the images drawn.

Who will make the porcupine pop? This exciting kids' game combines simple play with silliness and suspense. Roll the die, press the porcupine's nose that number of times, and hope his darts don't fly out. No one knows when that will happen, and the only player not to make the porcupine pop wins. Kids can switch up the gameplay and try a round where whoever makes him pop first wins. Or try a game where each dart color is assigned points and when the porcupine pops, players race to grab as many darts as they can, and the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

The irreverence Blanc-Manger Coco now explore the theme of LGBT + culture, published in partnership with the magazine Têtu.

Mix the cards or not with the other games in the range: you decide.

Contains 160 reply cards and 40 question cards.
Shoot your balls into the holes to drop down and create a line of 4 balls before your opponent. Basically it's connect 4 with launchers, balls instead of disks, and simultaneous play.

In Tiki-Taka: a card game, two players take on the role of opposing football managers, using player and action cards to dominate the pitch and outscore their opponent. By carefully devising a strategy around their goalie, captain, tactical style, and players, a savvy manager can outwit and overwhelm the opposition. But every great manager must also monitor the flow of the match. Play too cautiously and you risk ceding goals before your team is at full strength; play too aggressively and you risk losing momentum just as the match reaches its climax.

Over four rounds of intense competition, players will go head-to-head, seeking to score more points than their opponent in each third of the pitch. Win a majority of these zones and you score a goal; win a majority and shut out the opposition completely, and you score two goals! The player with more goals at the end of the regulation 90' match (four rounds) is the winner.

Players win zones via points obtained by player cards and action cards. Player cards are drawn and drafted at match start and halftime, and are placed on the field for the duration of the match. Building a team with similar nationalities and complementary strengths is the foundation to success. Action cards are drawn and drafted at the start of every round and can be played only once for their points or other effects. But crucially, these cards may also be saved between rounds, forcing smart managers to carefully conserve their strength.

Just as in real football, the very best managers must blend the strengths and weaknesses of their squad, the tactical styles of both teams, and a keen sense of momentum to exploit the opposition and lead their side to victory. Are you up to the task?

—description from the designer

The Ambrosia Hotel is a Valley Falls historical landmark and the scene of an unsolved murder from the 1930’s. Local millionaire Harlan McAllister has purchased the hotel and spent the past 6 months restoring it. A week before the Grand Reopening he has invited a series of well-known Valley Falls personalities who all have historical ties to the hotel including famed mystery author Rosemary Clark (last seen in The Dark and Stormy Night). The night’s events are proceeding as planned until one of the guests, Sinclair LeCompt, doesn’t show up for dinner. After checking his room, they find that LeCompt’s door is chained from the inside. Once the chain is cut and access to the room is gained, LeCompt’s body is found. He has been stabbed through the heart. But how is such a thing possible? The only one of the guests with an airtight alibi is Rosemary Clark. After the Will Street Detective Agency is brought in to help with the case, they enlist her assistance with this locked-room mystery. Can you help Rosemary Clark and The Will Street Detective Agency solve the mystery of The Old and Hidden Secret?

The air has a chilly nip to it. Snow is falling lightly. The holidays are almost here. The Lamplight Theatre is in the middle of a run of performances of Dickens' "A Christmas Carol." Reviews have been good, and ticket sales have been even better. Yet, things in Valley Falls never stay happy and festive for very long. When Victor Day, the lead actor playing Ebenezer Scrooge in the performance, is found dead, the show's future is called into question. Evidence points to one of the members of the cast and crew being responsible. Can you solve the case of "Death at The Lamplight Theatre?"

Nightingale Lane is set off the main road and difficult to see from the highway. In other words, it's a perfect spot for secrets. When a courier for Coyote Parcel Service needs a signature for the envelope he is supposed to deliver to 148 Nightingale Lane, he peeks through the window in hopes of getting the owner's attention. What he sees is a room covered in blood-soaked plastic. Could there be a logical explanation for such a sinister sight? Is it possible that this isn't what it looks like? Or has he stumbled upon the lair of a madman who has managed to keep to the shadows and out of the public eye? Explore the house on Nightingale Lane and find out what's really been going on there.

When Jodie Wong hosts a group of her closest friends for an evening of fun, she decides the entertainment will be a game called The Circle that requires everyone to write down one anonymous fact about themselves. The group will then take turns guessing which fact corresponds to which person. She decides to make things even more exciting by having everyone type out their fact on an antique typewriter that was owned by a murderer named Nicholas Hundley. Things get weird, however, when there are more messages than participants and one of the notes reads, "I killed a person once. Tonight, I’ll kill another.” No one admits to writing the note, and everyone goes home thinking it was nothing more than a bad joke. It doesn't seem funny the next day when Jodie is found murdered in her home. Was Jodie Wong killed by one of those closest to her who attended the party? Or was the typewriter a conduit through which something sinister entered this world? Enter The Circle and find out.

The Valley Falls Museum is home to some of the world’s most exciting art exhibits and archaeological finds. It is also known for its wonderful ancient history curator, Albert McCain. However, everything changes when a successful heist of the museum results in McCain's death. Based on evidence recovered from the scene, authorities arrest fellow museum employee, Noah Parker, and charge him with robbery and murder. Noah is a friend and calls you immediately, desperate to proclaim his innocence and enlist your help. He is aware of the Will Street Detective Agency's stellar track record and is hopeful that you will find information that will exonerate him. Solve the mystery of the museum heist, the murder of Albert McCain, and collect the clues that could either free Parker from jail or lead to his conviction.

When famous Valley Falls hacker, Fluk3, infiltrates the computer database of The Will Street Detective Agency, the act doesn’t follow the usual pattern of anarchy and rebellion. After discovering information in a cold case that relates to the murder of a relative, it becomes necessary to make amends and enlist the agency’s services. Now offering assistance instead of resistance, Fluk3 offers to be the eyes and ears on the web in exchange for helping bring closure to this unsolved mystery.

Fairmont Estates is a tightly-knit neighborhood in the heart of Valley Falls. The residents are all white-collar and well-to-do, and any of the homes could be featured on the front of any picture postcard... except the one on Bradford Street. That house has been tied up in probate court after the previous resident passed away and has been vacant for the last three years. The house, understandably, has fallen into disarray and been vandalised on occasion by some of the neighborhood teenagers. When a family is finally able to purchase the home, the first thing they do is begin extensive renovations to return the place to its former glory. As demolition and construction are both underway, the owners are shocked when the remains of a reporter that has been missing for the past year are discovered behind one of the walls. Even more chilling is the fact that she seems to have been interred while alive which is confirmed by one final message she wrote that begins: “To whoever finds me, the Fairmont residents all have secrets. One of them did this to me...” .
One city. Two killers in competition. A twisted game that can only end in bloodshed.

Brutality, violence, an attempted prison break, death….or, in other words, just another day at Valley Falls’ most infamous correctional facility. In a place filled with criminals and guards who are just as vicious as the inmates they supervise, anyone could be a killer. The only question is who is responsible for the newest murder...and why did they do it?

Summary: Museum Nocturnus is a paranormal museum dedicated to cursed objects, items taken from haunted locations, serial killer artifacts, and anything else weird that wouldn’t stand a chance of being curated for any other museum. Valley Falls’ resident group of ghost hunters, S.T.A.L.K., purchased the museum in hopes of turning a profit and boosting their profile within the paranormal community. Father Richter Casey agrees to spend the night inside the museum and perform a series of blessings on the place before it reopens. Things go awry, however, when the priest is found dead the following morning. Given the frightful nature of the place, the list of potential culprits includes both human and supernatural suspects. Your job is to investigate The Hall of Murderers, The Hall of Cursed Toys, and The Hall of Haunted Items in order to determine what really happened to Father Casey.

Draft, develop and manage a minor league baseball team via write and roll mechanics in this print and play game.

You draft eight baseball players and spend a season developing their skills via dice rolls. Your team plays through a season, pitting the team's skills against dice checks at pivotal moments of each game. If all goes well, you contest the league championship at season's end.

Along the way, earn revenue by developing a player to your big league affiliate's needs, qualifying players for the All-Star game, winning the regular-season MVP and fending off injuries.

—description from the designer

Welcome to Usurper, a fantasy role playing game of adventure and storytelling.

Usurper is a fantasy adventure game of quick, light-hearted adventure. Using a simple table to resolve actions on, the game allows for various degrees of success, random events and unexpected outcomes without any complicated math.

Characters are described in plain English terms rather than lists of numbers and modifiers and can be created to taste in a matter of minutes with a notepad or through a random generation system if the player prefers to use that.

Usurper is meant to scale from street-level to epic campaigns deciding the fate of kingdoms.

Emphasis is on providing as many tools as possible to generate worlds, cities and adventure ideas, allowing pick-up games with as little preparation work as possible.

The rules cover the following aspects:


    Resolving actions with no math.



    Two character creation systems, one driven by the player and a random system.



    Narrative combat system.



    A game system that will result in your characters changing unpredictably as they adventure. The person that comes back to their home town is never going to be the same person that left.



    Tables covering ill fortunes such as physical injuries, gruesome deaths, mental trauma and sanity loss.



    Follower and henchmen rules, including loyalty, morale, recruitment.



    NPC's and monsters will fit on an index card.



    Random NPC generator, including motivations and relationships.



    World builder with random generators for world history, city factions, quests, terrain and dungeon themes.



    Travel and random encounter tables.



    Tables to conduct a living, unpredictable campaign with random events and outcomes.



    Adventure system that adjudicates the progress of the heroes when fighting to bring down an enemy, overthrow a system or change the world.



    Random monster and magic item tables.



    Narrative magic system.



    Random deities with an active interest in their followers.



    Lead armies into battle, treating each unit as a character in its own right.



    Rule a domain.



    Be eaten by a monster.



     * * * *


The random generators are of value to players of any roleplaying game, even if they do not use the Usurper rules and are made to be as generic as possible, permitting their use in a wide variety of fantasy adventure games.

Likewise, this is well suited for cooperative and solo players due to the wide range of randomly generated content that can be generated.

BeerHex is a game with bluffing elements using bottle caps as pieces. You can play it casually (even while drinking beer). The players take turns to place a bottle cap face down on the board. Under each cap, a disk with a faction symbol is attached by magnet. The faction may be that of you or your opponent. Since the bottle caps are placed face down, the players do not know which symbols their opponents have placed. The object of this game is to connect the opposite sides of your color with your symbols. There are two ways to obstruct your opponent's moves. One is to place your symbol on your opponent's route to block it. The other is to "attack". To attack, choose and reveal 3 facedown bottle caps, and if all three have the same symbols, remove them from the board. Thus, it is highly risky to simply place caps with your symbol in a straight line to connect your sides, because the caps may be attacked and removed by your opponent!

-description from designer

Players make their way around the board as they accrue money and invest it in stocks, responding to positive and negative developments along the way. The first player to reach $1 million wins. If players end the game before someone crosses the $1 million mark, the player with the most money in their portfolio wins. The game is undated, but companies like "e Internet Solutions" to invest in and references to technologies such as a $4,000 handycam suggest the game was published in the late 1990s or early 2000s.

English:
Discover 4 new characters with new Gameplay for more discoveries, tricks and deep interactivity. Play as jack Rackham the Seducer, Charlotte De Berry the Indomitable, Charles Vanes the Unstable and Henry Jennings the Corsair!

French:
Découvrez 4 nouveaux personnages avec de nouveaux Gameplay pour toujours plus de découvertes, de fourberies et une interactivité poussée. Incarnez jack Rackham le Séducteur, Charlotte De Berry l’Indomptable, Charles Vanes l’Instable et Henry Jennings le Corsaire !

Schnellen; is a trick taking game with trumps. It is a popular card game in the region of Tyrol, South Tyrol and Vorarlberg. It is played with a set of German-suited playing cards (33 cards) where the 6 Bell-card (Weli) is always trump color and the second highest value. Each player gests five cards and is predicting the number of tricks taken by himself. If you succeed you loose points, if you fail you gain points.
There are variants with exchange (bad) cards and leaving a round.

—user summary

A trivia card game with questions related to Iceland in three categories; history, land and nature, people and culture. Players can choose two ways to play, ie. On The Road where they discard cards or Go Fish to collect cards, both times when players answer correctly.

—user summary

Frægð og frami (Fame and Glory) is a party game where players take on the role of an influencer who competes with his opponents to be the first one to gain 20 thousand followers. Each round player roll a die to move their pawn around the board and act as the spaces indicate, if needed. This gives the players the chance to add fame and glory cards to their hands, defend from attacks, add or lose followers and so on. When all players have rolled the Spotlight starts which is the task the influencers have to deal with. What does one not do to gain fame and glory?

—user summary

Players describe the key word without using the five forbidden words on the card, in one minute. Those who guess correctly get the card (a medal) while the president (current player) gets as many cards (medals) from the back of the box. After everyone has been president the one with most medals wins the game.

—user summary

Feel yourself in the role of the tycoon of the financial world of the early twentieth century! Trade with partners, buy enterprises, unite them into trusts and corporations! To win, develop strategic thinking, cunning, but most importantly - the flexibility of the mind and the ability to negotiate.

A great game for both a family vacation and a fun company from 2 to 12 people!

—description from the publisher

On their turn, players can buy one enterprise from the market by paying a certain combination of different currency cards. Players can exchange currencies among themselves to collect the desired combination. The goal of the game is to collect as many business cards from one industry as possible. The game ends when the market runs out of all company cards and then the players count the points for the cards they have purchased. Each enterprise card brings a certain number of points to its owner, but if he has several cards of the same industry, then the points brought by enterprises in this industry are higher, and if the player has collected all the cards of one industrial branch, he receives the largest possible number of points from these enterprise cards for Monopoly.

—user summary

Morph is a simple but challenging set collection, pattern matching game with the goal of being the first player to create a number of sets of 3 cards with the same shape. To do this, you must morph your hand by matching a card by color or shape with a card on the table. If you matched a card by color, you must match said card to a second one by shape, and viceversa.

—description from the designer

boardgame: War for Chicken Island: The Cackle of Cluckthulhu
description:
Chicken Island has been immersed in war for years now. Seeking for new powers in places where no chicken was meant to go, a new threat rises: The Cult of Cluckthulhu. Silent wanderers are called to a profane celebration. Their goal: to invoke unthinkable horrors out of this world or any other! Now, the Leaders of the chicken army must become Chickenstigators to fight together this dread terror or perish in darkness.

The Cackle of Cluckthulhu is a cooperative scenario for War For Chicken Island for 1 to 4 players.

Suggested by backers, created by Draco Studios & Detestable Games.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Incredible Voyages: Undersea Combat in the Victorian Age
description:
Enter the world of Incredible Voyages, Long Face Games’s fast play set of Victorian science fiction submarine rules. Where  the  undersea navies of the world’s Great Powers fight amongst themselves – and with anarchists, criminal gangs and evil arch-scientists for supremacy in a secret undersea war.

The rules are based on David Manley’s popular and successful “Stingray” game adapted and greatly expanded to cover the Victorian era. 
The rules use a hex movement system, but “free movement” rules are also included for those that don’t like hexes.

Use whatever models you want, go wild with your scratchbuilding, or download free 3D printing files online.

—description from the publisher

It's time for a mudbath! Drop Peppa into the puddle and try to splash mud on characters around the puddle. You will get a prize token for every succesful splash. First to splash mud on every character is the winner of the game!

Includes a challenging game for more experienced gamers.

Players admire the pyramids on the Mokattam Plateau in Egypt. When entering the great pyramid of Giza, a scary thing happens. The stone slab blocking the entrance to the pyramid closes with a huge bang. Whoever wants to get out of the captivity of the pyramid must answer the questions correctly. The cards feature questions from players on a variety of topics: science, technology, flora and fauna, literature, cinema, and the fine arts. Whoever answers the question correctly can move with his piece as many squares on the pyramid fields as the question card gives. Within the pyramid, you can move in any direction through the corridors, but you must not cross the walls and doors. The doors can only be crossed by answering a door opener card, but it is by no means certain that the door will be open and allow the adventurer to pass. When a player gives a correct answer to a door opener question, the door is revealed and you will know whether that door is open in any direction or if it has been blocked for a long time by a stone collapse. With the Szabadulj kvíz game, everyone can have fun, gain new knowledge, test their knowledge and learn many interesting things!

Hello and welcome, owners, to the genesis of a new league. An exciting and surprising evolution of football awaits. You will all take part in the making of great sports franchises over many seasons. Compete with other owners by drafting, trading, equipping, and improving your team. Who will rise to the top of the pantheon? Only time will tell!

Pigskin Pantheon is a 1-4 player football management card game.  Sign players ranging from chicken men, werewolves, sentient shovels, and even a dog on a skateboard.  Combine players with gear and accolades to dominate on game day.  If you're into it, sabotage opposing teams with scandals, gruesome injuries, and poaching rival players!

—description from the designer

It's the 1920s, and before these new airlines people are talking about can start flying around the world, the routes the aeroplanes will fly have to be mapped out and tested. As a daring pilot, you want to be the first, you want to set the record for the fastest flight between two cities, to be known as the Route-Finder — and the most challenging flights are intercontinentals: London to Melbourne, London to Wellington, or Tokyo to Rome.

But, if you're going to win fame and (perhaps) fortune, you need to be a careful planner and a dedicated engineer. Your aeroplane will have limited performance, so your route must be carefully chosen, and your aeroplane design well thought out to complete the route as quickly as possible. Before getting stuck into the fun stuff, you need to find the funding to support your flight – you think it’s a great idea, but how many others do? Luckily, it’s a time of great interest in flight and pilots, so finding some funding shouldn’t be a problem. Finding enough funding, though – that’s tricky. And then, Mother Nature awaits. Will she look kindly on your route-finding flight?

Game play in Route-Finder: Intercontinental involves using 2D6 and look up tables to find your sponsors and Finance, which allows you to buy the parts for your aeroplane. Choosing these depends on the route you intend to fly - will it be land or water based, short or long hops? But you need to track how much Finance you've used - you'll need a lot during the flight for refuelling, repairs, etc. Once your aeroplane's ready to go, your flight is a series of flight legs and city stopovers, with your aeroplanes technologies and 2D6 rolls and look up tables determining what happens during each. If you manage your Finance right, keep your aeroplane in good order, and choose your Route wisely, you might just find your Route.

Multi-player mode can be co-operative or competitive. Several players can play as different members of the same crew, each bringing different strengths to the challenge, and sharing in the decision making. Or each player could fly their own aeroplane, the pilot who arrives in the least number of Days being the winner.

boardgame: Felicitas Fogg: In 80 Karten um die Welt
description:
Felicitas Fogg is a point-to-point movement bidding game with augmented reality (AR) support, where players have to select vehicles from their hand to move the character Felicitas Fogg on her trip around the world or discard them from their hand to restore energy and draw two new cards. The vehicles have different values for range, energy consumption and data traces. The values are modified by events like weather effects and individual player abilities. Felicitas always chooses the vehicle with the highest maximal range and the player winning the bid gains victory points depending on the number of fields she travelled. However, a hacker from F1X collective chases her, so the players are forced to leave as few traces of data as possible. At each location switch, the chaser makes an attractive offer and players may individually choose to help him. But whenever he catches Felicitas, all players lose the game.

Felicitas Fogg has been published by the organization Deutschland sicher im Netz (Germany safe in on the Internet) and is sponsored by the German Federal Ministry of Transport and Digital Infrastructure.
publishers: Deutschland sicher im Netz e. V.

description:
Five new promo cards for Food Truck Champion originally included with the Crabs! Kickstarter Edition.

These crab themed cards are shuffled with the 95 game cards from the base game to augment that deck.  The cards play by the same rules as those from the base game, providing some new combinations of roles, recipes, and ingredients.
Players take turns stacking their tetris pieces on a shaking base. Whenever pieces fall off, the player has to take them. The first player that has placed all their pieces, wins the game.

boardgame: Of Silence & Shadow: A Dark Fantasy Skirmish Wargame
description:
Of Silence & Shadow is a skirmish wargame set in the realms of dark fantasy.

What is a skirmish wargame? 
A skirmish wargame is a type of wargame that uses relatively few figures compared to larger more boastful wargame battles. Skirmish wargames focus on individual models and their abilities, rather than large units of soldiers. 
In this game, each unit moves, acts, and fights on its own.

Dark fantasy is a type of fantasy where horror trumps the majestic. 
Where fire spells burn their casters and getting sliced by a sword causes more injury than just a few hit points. 
So, frame your mind around the darkest parts of fantasy, where life is horrific, knights do not always save the day, and princesses may be monsters in disguise.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Nguyen Hue '72: The 1972 Easter Offensive in Vietnam
description: Grand Strategic treatment of the 1972 campaign with aircraft and armor, artillery, infantry and helicopter minis, two big beautiful maps at 10 miles/hex, event cards every turn, weekly turns, ZOC, terrain and supply effects, US airpower, airmobile and amphibious movement, a real mystery unit dynamic, and max replay-ability.  1975 campaign 275 scenario included!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: The Walking Dead: All Out War – Chris, Leader of the Hunters
description:
The 2019 Event Exclusive for The Walking Dead is Chris, leader of the Hunters. Under Chris’ leadership, the Hunters have survived the horror of the new world by hunting the easiest prey — humans.

The 2019 The Walking Dead: All Out War Event Exclusive includes:

     1 plastic miniature,
     2 resin objectives
     Chris Survivor Card
     Webley WG army model, Gator Pro, Ka-bar combat knife, Flashlight, PCPs, Climbing gloves, Camo paint, and Jerry can Equipment Cards
     "Stab" and "Dirty fighter" Veteran skill cards
family: Game: The Walking Dead – All Out War

boardgame: Memory Kings: Extra Seasons Expansion Pack
description:
Featuring 4 entirely new sets of cards, this expansion adds the Harvest, Fishing, Hunting, and Plague Seasons to your game, allowing an even larger play grid, or different colors selection.

-description from designer

Simulate the legendary tank battle of Prokhorovka for the first time in a 1 to 1 fashion (every single tank, every single squad ...) on the real terrain, obtained from the original Lutwaffe air recon images!  This Advancing Fire game is also playable with the simple Starter Kit rules!  The game has been revised by historian Cristopher A. Lawrence, author of Kursk: The Battle of Prokhorovka.

boardgame: Maxim Gorki: The Siege of Sevastopol 1942
description:
This is Critical Hit's module in ASL-compatible format for the battles in the area of the Maxim Gorki defence works at Sevastopol in 1942.  The defences consisted of two 300mm twin gun turrets, together with supporting defences and an observation post all set on high ground with excellent fields of fire, and were intended to be a tough nut to crack.  This is what the Germans had to attack in 1942 in order to capture Sevastopol and ultimately the whole of Crimea.

As a module, it is not a complete game in itself but requires ownership of the Advanced Squad Leader (ASL) rulebook and Beyond Valor module as a minimum.  However, it is a very distinctive module with rules for previously-omitted areas such as fighting in the underground bunker complexes and with a map that makes you understand the size of the challenge to take on a well-prepared defence - these go beyond what is normally seen in ASL.

There are 12 scenarios ranging from small engagements to larger battles which will enable players to experience the struggle whilst attempting to capture (or defend) a significant fortification.

Story:

The largest base of the SCP Foundation has fallen. Its strict security measures, designed to keep paranormal objects at bay, have failed. Consequently, artifacts, entities, and phenomena beyond human understanding, recovered from all over the world, are no longer protected. Or contained.

You are a survivor of Site 19, the fortress, laboratory, and home to these anomalies. And now you must find a way to escape this mysterious place, where only a select few enter and even fewer leave. There will be unexpected challenges, unfamiliar enemies, and experiences you will never forget. That is, if you manage to survive...

About the game:

Escape from Site 19 (EFS19) offers a unique playing experience. Game participants do not compete against each other, but rather work together as team members to beat the game. As survivors of the containment breach at Site 19, the base of the international clandestine organization, the “SCP Foundation”, each team member must move along the game board to complete his escape. This ultimately leads to an encounter with SCP-0001, the most significant anomaly of them all.

Along the way, the team draws cards featuring the escaped anomalous objects, the SCPs. The cards reveal physical and mental tasks. At first, the tasks are relatively straightforward, but as the game progresses, they become increasingly difficult, requiring trust and teamwork.

Throughout the game, every team member will face challenges and share his stories, making Escape from Site 19 a meaningful and memorable experience.

️⚠️⚠️ This game may be difficult to play for squeamish gamers. Many tasks involve bodily fluids and/or parts, challenges in this game are focused on pushing your uncomfortability boundaries. You have been warned ⚠️⚠️
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
publishers: Extreme Board Games, s. r. o.

description:
Core Worlds: Nemesis is the official solo version of the Core Worlds deck-building game. Play against the AI-driven Nemesis Empire during your bid to conquer the Core Worlds. The Nemesis is represented by a special deck of cards that simulates play against a human player, including anticipating which worlds the Nemesis is targeting in each sector and which Core Worlds the Nemesis is planning to conquer as it drafts cards throughout the game.

—description from the designer

A worker placement and engine building-game about living a good life, with fully customizable personalities (more than 100,000 possible combinations).

This card-based worker placement game starts with you creating your own unique personality. Your personality consists of:

     Nature: A long-term ability you have for the entire game
     Nurture: A change to your character's starting stats
     Habit: A special ability you can use once per round
     Goals: A list of tasks that give you victory points each round if you achieve them


Each of these cards is double-sided, so after being dealt four of them randomly, you choose which sides of each card to use in order to mix and match them to create your own unique personality. For example, with one set of four cards you could be:

     An ignorant wealthy narcissist who has a habit of taking mental health days 
     A wise, but heavily in debt, narcoleptic who enjoys working with close friends
     Or any combination of the above


Once the game begins, players simultaneously choose actions using time as a resource, carefully selecting which opportunities will benefit them the most. Using these opportunities, players will influence their wealth, future opportunities, or emotional state, to build an engine that will result in the most Bliss by the end of the game.

—description from the designer

Astrocracy is a socio-political sci-fi game for 3-6 players. It is a competitive, deck building, worker placement, resource management game, where players act as the leader of a galactic nation, seeking to guide humanity in a common direction. Players draft from Ideological Stances that represent the culture of their nation. The combination of Stances selected determines a player’s starting Draw deck. Once the game begins, players will assign their Population to work their planets, yielding the planet’s resources. Players will then Draw their cards and take their actions, such as constructing androids, trading with other players, patronizing Great People, discovering Technologies, Surveying new worlds. Other phases include feeding your people, voting on galactic policies, tracking the happiness of your people, and much more.

—Rick Wolford, game's designer

滅茸 (Metsutake) is a bluffing card game from Japan about growing mushrooms.

Players try to grow and eat the best mushroom, but people risk falling ill if they eat the wrong kind of mushrooms. The players are encouraged to eat wild mushrooms over those grown in farms. If they happen to feed poisonous mushrooms to other players, well accidents happen.
user summary
artists: Soya Okamura (オカムラ ソーヤ)

description:
In Simon's Cat: Dinner Date, the deck is composed of pictures with birds and special "Simon's Cat" cards. Players draw cards and lay them in a row. The first player to find the right bird and point to it gathers all the challenge cards and keeps them. Players guessing incorrectly return a card to the deck. When a special Simon's Cat card comes up, the first player to shout "Simon's Cat!" wins the card for extra points at the end of the game.

The game stops when all the cards in the deck have been used. The player with the most points wins.

boardgame: The Taverns of Tiefenthal: Open Doors
description: The Taverns of Tiefenthal: Open Doors consists of four modules for use with the base game, with players now having the option to add a wine cellar or a guest room to their tavern. The innkeepers give players individual skills that might influence their strategy in the game, and the mayor will reward players when you help him to develop "his" town into a tourist center.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
POT SMUGGLIN' BLUES consists of a board map of the Caribbean, a spinner that drives the game, four HOT WHEELS that locate weather conditions and police hot spots, and four sets of CARDS that determine weather conditions, mechanical difficulties and other fortunes & (Mis)Fortunes. Your goal is to sail your load of marijuana to a North American port and payola.

Except when you are safely in a North American port, every move begins by spinning the spinner. Each number on the spinner also contains 6 colors.

When a player spins, the number and color landed on not only select the load amount offered and country of origin, they also rotate one of the Hot Wheels. The number spun tells you how far to move your sailboat or determines when you've encountered Mechanical Difficulties or a (Mis)Fortune. The compass direction you've spun sets the wind direction.

>- Decide who goes first and have each player select a colored boat stand.
>- Everyone's first spin determines their load's quantity, quality, and country origin. The number spun is the number of thousands of pounds you are smuggling (2 on the spinner means 2,000 pounds). When you decide to take a load, borrow your stake, and place the appropriate sail, depicting the country of origin and the quantity of pot, onto your boat.

You will face challenges with rough weather, mechanical difficulties, avoiding the US Coast Guard, getting through US Customs, and the Bermuda Triangle.

The Pot Smugglin' Blues board game is drawn from true-life experiences of a bygone era. Will Berger’s true-life experiences. Practically everything that can happen playing the game actually did happen to Will Berger while sailing the Caribbean back in the 1980's. In those precartel, pre-terrorist days, smuggling marijuana was considered romantic adventure, not to mention a fantastic way to create great stacks of fast cash, and in the process encounter informers and snitches, get busted, lose all your money and go to jail with the blues. All of which conspires to make the Pot Smugglin' Blues game suspenseful, emotion-filled escapism.

So what are you waiting for? Load your sailboat with the good herb, plot your course north for a safe passage, hoist sails and find your wind, get out the star charts and see the Southern Cross for the first time, deliver the weed to the promised land and reap millions of dollars for your success. Do it now-- when they legalize pot, the thrill will be gone. Smooth sailing!

Call by some NICKNAME and ask for a Item card. It's fun when you meet someone for the first time.
"nickname" was born out of the thoughts of middle school girl "Nanana Kudo".

[Rules]
Pick any player who seems to have the card you want and collect it by guessing.
This is a game where you collect four Item cards from the same nickname group.

    Close to rules called Author, Quartet


When you ask for help, you call any your opposition under they's first name such as "Doctor", "Teacher", "Leader" and "C.E.O." which are written on a Item card that you want.

"DR. Albert, can I have a FLASK?"
"TEACHER John, give me a TEXTBOOK. Please."
"C.E.O. Steve, Can I borrow your LUXURY CAR?"

That's how each of you call each other in different ways during the game.
And the player with the most nickname groups wins.

Zombie Circus Goats is a 2-4 player game. In it players start with three cards and then, in turn order, draw a single card. Players may play talent cards and then roll dice to collect gold, brains, blood or guts cards based on their talent and roll.

The game includes a trading mechanism where players can trade talent cards with one another. The winner is the first player to amass 5 zombie circus goat cards.

—user summary

Zombie Circus Goats is the strategic new card game about everyone's favorite things:  Zombies, Goblins, Goats, and Shouting.

Build your troupe.  Steal your friends' Goats.  Howl in victory!

WARNING:  Contains Hellephants, Two-Headed Boys, Robotic Bees, Weregartners, Ma'ampires, Rabbits w/ lightsabers, Meerkats, Tiny Sasquatch, Arrogant Octopuses...and a whole bunch of Zombies.

Get Paid in Body Parts.

Shout in Bad Accents.

Cast Weird Spells at Your Friends.

Make Felicia Fetch You a Drink.

For 2-6 live players.  30-90 minutes adjustable playtime.  Host nights of howling infamy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Company of Heroes: World Builder Pack
description:
The World Builder Pack provides 2 double sided map panels (Summer River and Winter Plains). These maps have detailed art but have been left primarily open so they can be freely modified. This pack includes 48 double sided terrain tokens that allow you to modify the existing maps of the Core Set or create new ones. These components will be very useful in using the online map and scenario releases planned for after launch.

The winter side maps will tile well with Stalingrad and Hill 331 while the summer maps will tile with Trois Ponts and Monastery.

This set introduces unique structures that provide benefits to units inside as well as elevation features.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Zombie Circus Goats: +2 Players Expansion Pack
description:
+2 Players Expansion Pack is a family-safe expansion for Zombie Circus Goats to support up to 6 players.

It includes:
7 more ornery Goats.
7 Rabbits doing things from movies.
7 Goblins frowning at things.
7 Meerkats in dangerous situations.
22 more Filthy Lucre cards.

boardgame: Zombie Circus Goats: NSFW 1 – The Offendening Expansion Pack
description:
NSFW 1: The Offendening Expansion Pack expands Zombie Circus Goats with 13 new NSFW cards.

Horrify your friends.  13 NSFW cards to make you argue, shout, and snort wine out your nose.

This expansion is included in:

Zombie Circus Goats 3000:  The Everything Box

boardgame: Zombie Circus Goats: NSFW 2 – Fine, I'm Leaving Expansion Pack
description:
NSFW 2: Fine, I'm Leaving Expansion Pack expands Zombie Circus Goats with 13 new NSFW cards.

Offend your father-in-law.  13 more cards to make you tear out your hair and light things on fire.

boardgame: Zombie Circus Goats: RWC 1 – Real World Consequences Expansion Pack
description: RWC 1: Real World Consequences Expansion Pack expands Zombie Circus Goats with 13 new, family-safe cards. Make your friends sing, cry, and Dance Monkey Dance.

boardgame: Zombie Circus Goats: Warning – There are Goblins Expansion Pack
description: Warning: There are Goblins Expansion Pack expands Zombie Circus Goats with 13 new, family-safe Goblins. Make your friends sing, cry, and Dance Monkey Dance.

boardgame: Zombie Circus Goats: Zombies Invade the Moon Expansion Pack
description:
Zombies Invade the Moon Expansion Pack expands Zombie Circus Goats with 13 new, family-safe characters.

Family safe.  13 ZCG characters with space helmets, aliens, and laser beams!

boardgame: Sleeping Gods: The Secret Cabal Gaming Podcast Promos
description: Two item cards for Sleeping Gods: Spider Bow and Supply Pack

boardgame: Zombie Circus Goats: The Family Box
description:
Zombie Circus Goats is a 2-4 player game. In it players start with three cards and then, in turn order, draw a single card. Players may play talent cards and then roll dice to collect gold, brains, blood or guts cards based on their talent and roll.

The game includes a trading mechanism where players can trade talent cards with one another. The winner is the first player to amass 5 zombie circus goat cards.

ZCG: The Family Box is a compilation which includes everything in the base game and the following items:

+ 2 Players Pack

RCW 1: Real World Consequences
Warning:  There are Goblins
Zombies Invade the Moon
The Zombie Pizza! Token

boardgame: Zombie Circus Goats 3000: The Everything Box
description:
Zombie Circus Goats is a 2-4 player game. In it players start with three cards and then, in turn order, draw a single card. Players may play talent cards and then roll dice to collect gold, brains, blood or guts cards based on their talent and roll.

The game includes a trading mechanism where players can trade talent cards with one another.The winner is the first player to amass 5 zombie circus goat cards.

ZCG 3000: The Everything Box is a compilation which includes everything in the base game + the following expansions.

+ 2 Players Pack

RCW 1: Real World Consequences
Warning:  There are Goblins
Zombies Invade the Moon
The Zombie Pizza! Token
NSFW 1: The Offendening Expansion Pack
