You are an award-winning shepherd getting ready to win the annual Wool and Fleece competition. And this year, you are hunting for the best and shiniest sheep - but so are your fellow competitors. Be prepared to use all sheeps and sizes to claim victory.

All Sheeps and Sizes is a strategic card game about sheep. You want to get high scoring sheep into your sheep pen to give you points. The game ends when all the cards in the deck run out. If you have the most points at the end of the game, you win!

Your sheep pen is like your hand because it is where you keep and play all your cards from. But all the cards in your sheep pen are placed face down and you can't look at them so you will have to remember which card is which so that you can play the right cards.

Some of the cards in the game give you points. But the Wolf card reduces your points, so you want to make sure you don't have the wolf at the end of the game. The other cards have special effects to be used during the game, like viewing, swapping, stealing and destroying other cards. Use your cards effectively to win!

—description from the designer

In Trinity of Reign you take over the rule of one of the four clans and build your empire. Construct cities, castles and metropolises. Lead your capital to fame and power through rapid expansion. Recruit new warriors and send out your druids to develop new potions and consolidate your rule. Choose your path. Take reign either by creating wealth, by invoking the gods, or by conquering land.

Trinity of Reign is a strategy game for 2 to 4 players is aimed at casual to professional players. Easy to learn, it offers countless strategic possibilities and ways of playing. The detailed building miniatures and the possibility of individual expansion of the capital let you create a unique little world in every game, but you can never be completely sure of its integrity.

Win the game by one of the following three variants:

     By military conquest of two complete regions (12 provinces, the military victory can be obtained.
     By building the Hall of Ancestors in the capital, the wealth victory can be won.
     By preparing the potion of the highest god Taranis and using it by a druid, the religion victory can be achieved.


If no player achieves one of the above victory conditions within the maximum nine rounds, the game ends with a points victory for the player with the most points.

Each round contains of four different phases. It starts with the Earnings, where each player receives the tax revenue and mines his resources. Next comes the Actions of Druids, where the druids can collect ingredients, prepare potions or do other special actions. With the potions, they brew, you can unlock new special abilities for your druids. Then, in the Buying phase, any desired buildings, capital expansions, or units can be purchased. The capital expansions will give you special Bonis, towns or metropolises in the province, give you more tax income and with new troops you can conquer your opponents or protect your country from foreign invaders.  In the Place, Move & Fight phase, the acquired buildings or units are then placed on the map, then you can move your troops and solve battles if they occur.

—description from the designer

You headed west to make your fortune, but the situation is volatile. You’ve got to keep pushing your luck to improve your advantage and claim the bounty. But go too far and you may end up with nothing! Will you play it safe or risk it all on a big win?

Play is simultaneous. The game occurs over several rounds. On each round, one player rolls a single die multiple times. Players track the numbers rolled on the path around the edge of their scoresheet. Each time the die is rolled, the number thrown is added to the previous total.

If a 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 is thrown: After each throw, independently each player must decide whether to bank OR spend OR continue.

If a 6 is thrown: Any player still in the round busts. When you bust, cross off a bust box. You may not bank or spend any money. If you run out of bust boxes, you are out of the game.

Points in the bank will count towards your end game score. Points spent will give you benefits later in the game. Maybe you'll start higher up the track each turn, or score double on fives... The higher up the track you push, the greater the potential rewards, but the further you have to fall...

The winner is the player who banks the most by the end of the game.

Any number of players can play the game. Bank or Bust is also ideal for playing online via videoconferencing.

—description from the designer

The King of Lingo Land sets all visitors a task and only the highest performers are admitted. You’re under pressure and you need to impress. Five complete words must be created to secure your entry. The longer the word, the more you’ll score and topical words are even better. But beware, gobbledygook will get you nowhere!

Before you play, as a group choose a category (e.g., the ocean, food & drink or board games) and write it down.

Play is simultaneous. Each player chooses how to use the two dice on their own scoresheet. One die shows the box a letter must be chosen from and the other die shows the line that letter must be written on.

Each player writes their chosen letter anywhere on the selected line. Players may choose any letter from the box. Each letter can be used an unlimited number of times. Sixes are wild and can be used instead of any other number. Cross off a 'Pass' box if you choose not to place a letter.

Each player is allowed a maximum of three passes. After your third pass, you must withdraw from the game. Other players may continue until they have used all their passes. When the last player has finished, the game ends.

Your aim is to make a complete word on each line. You score points according to the length of the word, as shown on the scoresheet. If your word is relevant to the chosen category score two extra points.

To play, you’ll need to print a scoresheet for each player. You’ll receive the file in black and white, for easy printing. You may like to laminate your scoresheets and use dry erase pens to play. This will reduce your need to print new sheets out.

You'll also need 2 dice and a pen for each player. To play, you write directly onto the scoresheet.

Any number of players can play the game. Lingo Land is also ideal for playing online via videoconferencing.

—description from the publisher

You have inherited a palatial stately home, but sadly it’s in a terrible state of repair. The house is falling down and the grounds are being reclaimed by nature. You must tackle this fixer-upper before you can move in. Protect the historical features and cover up the worst of the blemishes to gain the most points.

One player rolls two dice. Play is simultaneous. Each
player plays at the same time on their own scoresheet. Each player chooses how to use the two dice. Other players may make other decisions.

One die shows the location (area) for the construction while the other shows the shape of the area that can be renovated.

Each player fills in their chosen shape type within their selected area. Players should aim to cover the squares containing the triangles and leave the squares containing the circles uncovered.

If you can't draw a shape, you must cross off a 'no throw' box. The game ends when someone has crossed off all three of their 'no throw' boxes. The player who is furthest through their renovation wins.

You just need 2 dice and a pen. To play, you write directly onto the scoresheet.

Any number of players can play the game. Palatial is also ideal for playing online via videoconferencing.

—description from the publisher

Your life-long dream of opening your own restaurant will soon become a reality. But first you must work on the menu, hire some staff, find a location, choose some fixtures and fittings and get the word out across the town. You have money in three different accounts at the bank. Use them wisely, or you may run out of money before you’re ready to open.

Play is simultaneous. On each turn, one player rolls all three dice and each player chooses how to the dice rolls on their own scoresheet.

1 die shows which numbered mini game you are playing this turn. If you choose a six for this die, you may pick any of the five games. 1 die shows what number you will use within that mini game and the third die shows the account you will spend money from in the bank.

The five different mini games all work in different ways...
In game 1 you are creating your menu - by adding elements to plates. In game 2 you are interviewing and hiring staff to work in your cafe. Game 3 enables you to choose the location of your restaurant. In game 4, you're able to select the best fixtures and fittings and create your own personal style, while game 5 helps you to get the word out to potential customers in the area.

Completing milestones within each games triggers bonuses - giving you extra turns and possibly creating a cascade of chaining actions. Players can also earn rosettes which give extra points at the end of the game.

Total up each of your mini games and add your rosette bonus to give your final score. Most points wins!

You just need 3 dice and a pen. To play, you write directly onto the placemat.

Any number of players can play the game. Restaurantrepreneur is also ideal for playing online via videoconferencing.

—description from the publisher

You’ve worked towards this moment for so long. You’ve spent countless hours on the menu, perfected the decor and spread the word far and wide… now it’s time to fling the doors open and welcome the first customers to your new cafe. But can you get the right food to the right customers at the right time? Or will unserved dishes pile up while fussy customers get increasingly hangry?

Play is simultaneous. On each round, one player rolls both dice. All players choose how to use the dice rolls on their own scoresheet. Each round represents a different time slot in the restaurant. There are three actions you may take on any turn:
1) Seat Customers, 2) Make Food, 3) Serve Food.

If you seat a '6' customer, you need to try to serve them a '6' meal, but you'll need a '6' waiter to serve the dish. Lower numbered customers give you fewer points but are easier to keep happy.

Serve breakfast food to breakfast customers before the brunch sitting starts. Score negative points for unfed customers and wasted food. Score double points for feeding special guests.

The player who has the highest score at the end of the game wins.

You just need 2 dice and a pen. To play, you write directly onto the placemat.

Any number of players can play the game. The Grand Opening is also ideal for playing online via videoconferencing.

—description from the designer

Próba Ognia (The Trial of fire) is a cooperative game with One versus Many mode. Players work together as a group of firefighters trying to overcome the Disaster.

Base game contains 3 different scenarios - one full cooperative (against Electricity) and two in One versus Many mode (against Fire and Flood).

During each of their turns, firefighters play two cards that allows them to perform actions: Move, Help, Scout or Fight. Firefighters move around the city, find dangers and victims and try to fullfill scenario goals, whereas Disaster plays card to give them stress, destroy buildings or kill victims.

Base game contains 90 cards for firefighters in 6 prepared decks, but also allows players to mix them up and create their own custom decks. More cards are going to be available in upcoming expansions and distribute during series of local events.

Game is based on real firefighters actions and was made in close cooperation with Orlen Foundation, that helps local volunteer fire deps.

-description from designer

In this game, players are pushing their luck and trying to find the exact right combination of fillings in their pie and serving it up.

The active player draws and rolls 2 more dice than the number of players from a bag with 10 dice (8 dice in 4 colors which represent the 4 different pies and 2 wild dice). 
Then he takes 1 of the rolled dice and marks the colored number of the dice or the corresponding amount of tiles of that colored pie on the player sheet (each pie has 8 tiles).
After the active player, the other players clockwise take 1 die until every player has selected 1 die. Then the next player becomes the active player and starts a new round with new drawn dice.
There are some (one-time) actions to influence the dice rolls and results like change the the chosen die value +1/-1, add a die from the bag and reroll.
The game ends when one player completed all 4 pies or all 6 colored numbers of 1 pie on the player sheet.
Scoring is done by sum up the marked numbers of the pies, which give points influenced by the pie completeness, and the player with the highest point total wins.

Solo mode is mostly played the same way, but has an additional end-game trigger when none of the 3 rolled dice can be used to mark a number or pie tiles.

A tiptoi quiz card game about music and instruments.

The four assistants "Käpt'n Quizzo" (KQ), "Jonny Joker" (JJ), „Wilma Weg“ (WW), and "Robert Ross" (RR) guide through the game.

Knowledge: Learn about music and Instruments as depicted on 27 different cards.

Trivia game: Players answer KQs questions and score victory points. KQ announces the winner at the end of the game.

Electronic: You need an electronic Tiptoi pen for the game.

A tiptoi game to learn to spend your pocket money and calculate.
One makes one to three moves on the city map, enters either stores to spend the pocket money on food, sweets or toys and learnS to calculate the change or explore the city to gain more money to spend. The first player to buy objects of all three categories and enters the park on the playing board wins.

This is an expansion booklet for The Deluge, 1940: The Fall France, and Fire in the Steppe (all three needed to play).

We look at the tanks that the Poles tried to acquire or produce before the German invasion. Polish industry wasn’t capable of putting the new tanks into series production in time, and the Poles failed in their quest to purchase the Somua S35.

That’s not the case in Bron Pancerni. The Poles get their S35’s, and they also get the 25TP medium tank with a long-barreled 75mm gun, the 14TP cavalry tank with a long-barreled 47mm gun, and the 10TP and 9TP light tanks: the armor you need to stomp the szkopy.

These are very good tanks for 1939; they’re still pretty good in 1941, too. You get 24 new die-cut and silky-smooth playing pieces and new scenarios to use them in battle. The Germans can get the T34 shock two years early; the 25TP is just as capable a fighting machine as the legendary Hammer of the Proletariat (and it’s crewed by Poles).

-description from publisher's website
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements

description:
In the near future, humanity renews itself. Economies revive and nations look again to the stars as humanity rediscovers the urge to reach beyond the confines of planet Earth. Collectively, Earth’s nations and people set a course for the exploration of the solar system and exploitation of the resources it has to offer. Together, they set forth the Edict. The Edict is a demand and a licence for the most capable to venture into space under the mandate of harvesting extra-terrestrial resources for the enrichment of the people of Earth. Five organisations race out into the solar system to discover and capitalise on the raw materials buried within the planets, moons, asteroids, and debris beyond Earth’s heavens. Who among them will gain ascendency in a race to establish a commercial empire in answer to the Edict?

You control one of these powerful organisations. Your aim is to explore, exploit and expand your influence into the solar system and export back resources essential to Earth. Your organisation’s capabilities and options are dependent on the executives you recruit to manage your Mission Control.

The winner of the game is the player with the most Victory Points at the close of Earth’s final Edict. Victory Points are earned through the accumulation of Solar Credits as well as achievements in scientific brilliance, political might and supremacy of exploration. By colonising new worlds, investing in ground-breaking technologies and recruiting the most distinguished minds in space exploration you will drive your organisation to control the stars and pave the way for new frontiers of humanity.

The game is advanced by fulfilling the demands of Earth as displayed on the Edict card, which are met by returning resources to Earth. Players take turns clockwise to use one of the four Mission Control departments that lists available actions dependent on the associated executive. Departments are improved by recruiting new executives. The recruitment round starts after each Edict is complete and gives all players an opportunity to bring in new talent.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Rapa Nui: Clan Wars consists of two new elements for the Rapa Nui base game: "The Sign of the Great Ancestors" introduces a new large obsidian Moaï, and seven Ahu tiles can be used to reinforce the importance of turn order while significantly lengthening the game.

2 player game:
Each turn the players put 1 token on the game board. When the players have placed 4 tokens each the moving phase begins. The players move in turns 1 of their tokens 1 space along the drawn lines. The first player to complete 1 of 36 possible winning figures wins the game.

4 player game:
The players play in teams with 2 tokens each. Communication limits may be agreed on.

3 player game:
Each player has 3 tokens and there are winning figures for 3 tokens.
 Solo game:
There are 16 tokens marked with a letter and some problems to solve with as few moves as possible.

This game was probably published about 1920.

—user summary

Verbagame is a fun game of changing words for 2-8 players. You can also play in different languages and their kindreds.

Played in two or more languages simultaneously.
(ITALIAN; ENGLISH; FRENCH; SPANISH; PORTOGUESE; DEUTSCH)

Create a different word every turn at the centre of the table, using the most cards possible to be the first player to run out of cards.

Each player places 16 tokens in his starting zone and holds 2 reserve tokens to place later in the game. Each token represents a special unit (e. g. tanks or planes) with it's movement and capture options printed on top.

The goal of the game ist to eliminate all opponent units or to enclose the units of the opponent till they aren't able to move.

The game was published in a paper envelope.

—user summary

The Anachrony Promo Pack contains every promo released for Anachrony, notably;
Anachrony: Doomsday Enhancement Pack
Anachrony: The Sentry Post 
Anachrony: The Board Game Spotlight Promos
Anachrony: The Secret Cabal 2019 Promo Cards

Race with your tape measure snail and be the first to collect all the flower petals.

At every turn, the player roll a dice and can unroll his measure tape snail to go to his color petals. The first player who collect all his petals and go back to the start point win the game.

The Tu'x Rebellion and is based on an entirely new IP.  It is a cooperative/competitive Medieval Fantasy Strategy Game of my creation.
In the base game two to four players take on the roles of Noble Houses fighting for the King, gaining favor by winning battles and proving themselves worthy administrators in the midst of a Civil War. Players work together to overcome the rebels while competing with your allies to earn the most favor from the throne.

Key Features:

     Factions:  Pick from one of four houses to play as during the campaign (House Kel, Lotan, Shobal, or the 7th Corps). Each Noble House has their own individual and unique strategy.
     Base Building:  increase your power and prestige by constructing military, defensive and economical buildings that can gain access to greater resources and higher quality troop types as they are upgraded.
     Resources:  As you expand influence and improve your land, you gain access to more resources. These need to be properly handled in order to bolster your power, help you grow and gain favor with the King.
     Army Recruiting:  Create your own army from core loyalist warriors and elite house troops.  Customize your army according to your vision. Train units to meet battlefield demands. Cooperate by sharing units through alliances to defeat the rebels and unite the loyalist factions.
     Political intrigue:  Internal and External events can quickly change the fortune for you and your fellow loyalists.  Manipulate these events to further your own goals while hindering those of your rivals.
     Battles:  Wage war against the rebels in skirmishes as small as five units or up to a massive battle of forty units.  Gather your allies to form large armies to bring the battle to the doorstep of the rebels.


The Tu'x Rebellion is a card-based strategy game similar to other customizable trading card games with battle tactics similar to tabletop miniature games. Players take turns simultaneously.  Each round is divided into phases; draw, build, conflict and discard.  Simultaneous play means the game is exciting for everyone and no one is left waiting to take action.

Battles are resolved in the conflict phase. One round of battle is split into the: Set up, Draw, Infantry, Ranged, Cavalry, Movement and Discard phases.  Action continues for allied players and the player controlling the Rebel Army during the battle.

You win by reaching a certain threshold of favor with the King.  However, everyone loses if the loyalists are defeated twice in battle or any player loses all the troops under their command.  Players must constantly balance the competitive and cooperative aspects of the game.


—description from designer

"Astro Tussle" is a free-to-play template-based miniatures skirmish wargame set in space.  The ruleset can be used with any miniatures to enable skirmishes in your favorite sci-fi settings.

Inspired by other genre greats, X-Wing, Battlestar Galactica: Starship Battles, and Gaslands.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: キャトルミューティレーション (CATTLE MUTILATION)
description:
キャトルミューティレーション (CATTLE MUTILATION) is a co-operative memo-style game from Japan. The players are cattle farmers, trying to improve their stock, but aliens abduct and harm the cattle. The farmers must try to reconcile matters with the aliens in order to save their herd.

The game has tiles showing various flying saucers, and alien tiles showing a saucer with its levitation beam on one side, and human to alien handshake on the other, numbered 1 to 10.

A tableau of face-down tiles for the various flying saucers is surrounded by the tiles showing beams. As players turn up saucers, they can pay cow tokens to the abduction beams to move them about. If the players can turn up a matching pair of saucers, they can flip a beam saucer to its handshake side, and recover the cow tokens.

In this way, players try to build up peace with the aliens, by retrieving handshakes without sacrificing too many cows.
user summary
artists: Soya Okamura (オカムラ ソーヤ)

boardgame: アルス・ノタリア (Ars Notalia)
description:
アルス・ノタリア　～君は魔法陣クリエイター～ (Ars Notalia -You are a magic circle creator-) is a drawing game from Japan. Players are students of magic, having taken up a 4 week internship at a magical research company. Their goal is to improve their grades and hopefully gain full-time magic work.

Each week they complete a magical research on a particular. A spell is formed from a combination of two word cards (English included). Players use agreed symbols to represent the components of the spell (water, fire and so on). Each player draws a version of the spell from the symbols required.

At the end of the course, each player studies the spells drawn by the other students, and casts a secret vote on how well they have been drawn. Players are looking for how easily the drawing is to read, and how well it matches the requirements of the spell.

Once all the votes are cast, the scores are revealed and totalled up. The best score wins the game and a new job.

Players must try to draw out on paper a spell, decoratively and cleverly. But it must be recognisable as the spell, and not too difficult to read and interpret correctly. A spell that is too easy, or too obscure, should score lower marks than a more finely crafted work.

The game has been presented as a Print'n'Play, with the black & white files online under a password, being released for the low price of ¥ 500.
user summary

Darwin’s Journey: Collector’s Edition contains both the base game and the Fireland Expansion. This is a Kickstarter EXCLUSIVE edition and will not be available at retail. It includes the following additional content and deluxe components:

- 40 metal coins in two denominations (1s and 5s) with “aged” wash finish

publishers: 黑城堡桌游 Black Castle Games

description:
Charley Horse Derby is a strategic horse-racing game.

Players choose their jockey card and are dealt two strategy cards. The players race around the around the track for 1 1/4 mile. You can play strategy cards on your turn. You can also pick up cards off the track by landing on a "charley" which are played right away. Use the tools the track gives you to send other opponents back or propel yourself forward.

Ride the rails on the bends for a shorter race or take the longer outside tracks to grab cards to jeopardize your opponent.

During the race players will encounter bears, deer, goats, snakes, and murder hornets while dealing with collusion, elbowing, impeding and broken legs. Then there's those nasty "trade places" which you have to use before someone crosses the finish line.

You collect cards by rolling doubles or by landing on an opponent and some cards allow you to steal a card from a player. If you can steal an opponent's jockey card, they can't cross the finish line without one. Crossing first doesn't guarantee a win since all players get to finish their turn. Horses unfortunately die on the track so we added one death card. You have be careful using it because if the opponent neighs it, you're dead. Neigh cards are used to block actions of an opponent or have them miss a turn or to save your own hide.

The cards coupled with the track makes for a hilarious game where the horses are all over the place. You won't know who wins until the last player goes.

—description from the designer

In Vive la France ! each player gets to play a faction and to sets up plots, each more improbable than the next, defend them and then try to impose them.
Fast, simple and fun .

Once the Factions are assigned, the game can begin!
It is broken down into several game turns and phases.
Firstly - players have 4 turns to create conspiracy theories (using memes on the cards).
Then they have to present their conspiracy, remembering as many memes as possible from the previous phase. They score a point for each one.
The players will then use their faction cards to hire some personalities to get special advantages for the next phases.

And so on until one player gets enough points to win.

El juego de Vitoria-Gasteiz, a Monopoly-like game that invites you to walk the streets of the capital of Alava, buy and invest in some of its 57 local businesses.

In this game, the winner is the first player who acquires seven venues.

The board is a track from Square 1 to Square 63. First to reach the last square wins. Movement is governed by a die roll. Throwing a 6 entitles you to another throw. When you land on the special squares you must follow the instructions. Most of these tell you to play a certain tune on your recorder. If played correctly you get another throw. To win you must throw the exact number to land first on the final square.

—user summary

Expansion to Castillo del Terror.

Includes:

     new monsters with abilities and powers.
     It also includes a new cooperative game mode.
     one new kid card to play up to 5 players

Play with just your partner or with other couples in two different gameplays in this fun and engaging game! Spark meaningful conversations and learn new things about your partner or team up together to compete against other couples to see who really knows each other best.

TWO GAME CHOICES - Connect one-on-one your partner or play together with other couples in two different gameplays.
QUALITY TIME WITH YOUR PARTNER - Get to know each other better as you dive into meaningful conversations and perform funny challenges - perfect for date night.
COMPETE AGAINST OTHER COUPLES - Team up with your partner and play with other couples to see who really knows each other best.
BUILD DEEPER CONNECTIONS - The simple gameplay and easy rules allows you to quickly engage with each other.
REPLAY VALUE - 200 playing cards guarantees hours of replayability. Perfect gift for him and her - makes a great wedding, newlywed or anniversary gift.

ガムトーク (Gumtalk) is a game where you tell story about a theme you're given.

There's a deck of cards. There are 6 themes on the front side of the card. There's a number on the back side of the card. You draw 2 cards to choose the theme for the game. Once the theme is given you tell a story that is related to the theme.

The story doesn't need to be something entertaining or interesting. You can talk about anything as long as it is somewhat related to the theme you're given.

Other players who listened to the story says "That's a good story" once the story is ended.
publisher's summary
artists: Soya Okamura (オカムラ ソーヤ)

description:
It has been known for a long time that in nature everything has its own order.  The fox will eat a hen, and the hen will eat good grain or worm.  Each animal just needs to eat its dinner.  But a hungry wolf is a very bad wolf that can eat someone else's grandma.  Sometimes you have to hunt for dinner, sometimes it is enough to put the muzzle to the ground, and sometimes you can eat up… waste.

What would the world look like backwards and what can a freak eat?  Check it out by playing "Eat Me!"

"Eat me!"  is a card game which, thanks to its funny convention, will familiarize the youngest players with the rules of consumption in force in nature.  The players arrange the "food chains" from the cards in accordance with the rules of nature - predators eat herbivores, and those, as vegetarians, eat plant food.  The game is enriched by action cards, which, for example, undo the player's movement, or the order of placing cards.  The person who gets rid of all cards in their hand wins.  The manual includes two additional gameplay variants: "Menu for the whole week" and "Everyone is very hungry".

In the co-operative game Kakerlacula, players need to light all the candles in the rooms of the haunted castle — but Kakerlacula is dashing through the hallways trying to scare the children and force them to leave.

Players start the game with a number of garlic tokens, and on a turn, you launch one of the cockroach tokens on a catapult to see how it lands: Can you walk through the corridors, or will the cockroach scurry from one symbol to the next? If the cockroach crosses your path, you lose a garlic token. Can you complete your mission before you run out of garlic?

boardgame: Battlestar Galactica: Starship Battles – Starbuck Promo
description:
Kara Thrace "STARBUCK" promo from the Game Boy Geek Season 8 Kickstarter.

New Lieutenant with + 3 to hit modifier.

Talents:
- Daredevil
- Nimble Pilot
- Quick reactions
- Towing
- Marksman
- Troubleshooter
- Inspiring
- Religious

Flaws:
- Alien Spacecraft

Note: It is the same card as found in Battlestar Galactica: Starship Battles – Starbuck: Captured Raider, but without the ship.

A pathogen is causing a pandemic! Scientists are mystified as to which gene is causing global fatalities, provoking you to start a quest to uncover the pathogenic gene sequence. The genetic processes of mutation and indels hinder your discovery progress. Utilise your intellect, together with the powers of base sequencing and CRISPR base editing technologies, to correctly reveal the pathogen gene sequence. Crack the pathogenic code to enable scientists to develop a vaccine to save humanity from an impending health crisis.

During each turn, players have to guess a hidden part of the pathogen genetic sequence. With every guess, the player is granted information about how accurate their guess was. Using deduction and reasoning skills, player record this information and use it to get closer to cracking the true genetic sequence!

Each turn, players can opt to use one of their wildcards, which can either help (Helper cards) them or hinder (Hinder cards) their opponents discovery progress. Play these cards wisely though, as you are only limited to a couple per game!
A player wins when they crack the full hidden genetic sequence!

Can you crack the genetic code?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Maiden's Quest: Snuzzler Dragon Savior Promo Card
description:
Maiden's Quest: Promo – Snuzzler Dragon from the Game Boy Geek Season 8 Kickstarter

New Savior Card with one gem on the Left behind side and 2 gems and downgrade a haste on the Saved side

boardgame: Maiden's Quest: Morgan of Draknor Savior Promo Card
description:
Originally available from the Game Boy Geek Season 8 Kickstarter.

New Maiden Card.  She has one Up icon on the front and her Flip ability pulls a Savior or Discovered Treasure from the currently Cellared cards into the Fan.

We are in the 800s. The Anglo-Saxons of Bretland are living peaceful lives, unaware of the imminent danger that is coming. Hundreds of Warriors and Shield Maidens from all over Scandinavia are sailing for the mainland, setting foot for the first time ever on English soil. Led by the most powerful Viking leaders, they set about raiding and pillaging everything in their way. Their goal is simple: Gather as much wealth, land, and fame as possible to become the next Great Viking Leader.

Sagas: Bretland is a game for two to four players where each player forges their saga as they raid Bretland in search of wealth, land, and fame.

Starting with a small raid party on the shores of Bretland, each player will explore the land, expand their colonies, gather resources, and raid Anglo-Saxon settlements while battling any other Viking leaders that may stand in their way.

At the end of the 8th round, the player with the most victory points wins Sagas: Bretland. These can be earned by building new Viking villages, raiding existing Saxon settlements, raiding other player’s settlements, and completing specific objectives.

Who will be the next Great Viking Leader?

—description from the publisher

脱走名人 (Dasso Meijin) is a game from Japan about escaping pet lizards. Each player is a different pet lizard which has got free from its tank in the game designer's home (the game title means 'escape master'). They run around the rooms and try to catch insects. The first lizard to get a set of insects is the winner.

The game designer (who breeds pet lizards) describes the game as a mix of roll & move and Old Maid, in that the lizards follow paths around, and steal cards off each other akin to Old Maid.

However, the game does not use dice. Instead, players choose a numbered card from their hand in secret, reveal it and advance as far as they wish up to the number. The lowest numbers go first.

Players follow paths around the different rooms in the home, and try to catch insects in the rooms or collect useful objects (by drawing cards). If a player gets into a room with another lizard, they can steal a card from the other player's hand. But some cards are also traps.

The pet owner is also moving about, and if a player ends up in the same room, they cannot move or they will be caught. So being able to predict the movements of the other players and the owner matters.

The first lizard to secure a hand of four cards of one type of insect is the winner.
user summary

description: The Dirty Politics Module adds 24 trickery and take that cards to the game. It was included in the original Campaign Trail base game through stretch goals, and became a separate module with the release of the second edition.

The Gestapo deck adds 9 new, unique events, which make the game more challenging for experienced players.

Use this deck instead of the event deck that ships with the game.

Advanced Solo Mode is a 50-card deck featuring new rules, new depth and solo mode debates. It can be used in both solo and multiplayer games (with humans). Each advanced solo deck acts as one AI opponent; multiple decks can be used in a game.

The Advanced Solo Mode deck offers an easy, medium, and hard mode. The standard solo play deck (contained in each base game) does not include debates and is approximately medium mode.

Flame Demon levels up! This limited mini-expansion includes a special class cards and an amazing metal Flame Demon cube that replaces the standard wooden cube included in Dungeon Drop: Dropped Too Deep!

Additionally, this pack includes a metal Draglin Flagon and a special Flame Demon feat card to be used in Tavern Tales!

boardgame: La Résistance: Mes Amis ! C'est La Guerre Deck
description:
Mes Amis ! C'est la Guerre cards can be purchased in-game by spending a sum of dice that is 6 or greater. 
These cards give players advantages and/or allow you to mess with your opponents, adding a fun "Take that!" element to the game.

The base game ships with its own C'est la Guerre cards. 
The Mes Amis deck includes new cards such as American tanks, British commandos, and Allied planes. 
A big help, and big fun.

Mes Amis C'est la Guerre is a 9-card deck.
family: History: Resistance during WW2 in Europe

description:
Introduces 2 new game modes to Dungeon Drop, each of which allows the game to be played with 5 players for the first time! This expansion includes 9 heat printed 10mm cubes, 6 game cards, 5th player initiative token, hero meeple, scoring token and a rulebook.

New game modes:

- Sinister Spells: This competitive game mode allows all players to utilize the new spell cubes against one another. Drop a cube into the dungeon on another player's turn to petrify one of their abilities, syphon their life, or open an infernal gate allowing an EXTRA huge monster to emerge! There are 9 different spell effects, each represented by a unique heat-printed 10mm cube. Plays with 2 to 5 players!

- Wicked Wizard: This game mode introduces the concept of "1 vs. many" to Dungeon Drop. One player  takes on the role of the Wicked Wizard, wielding their devious spells in hopes to keep the locations of their Sinister Spellbooks hidden and maintain their dark grip on the dungeon. Meanwhile, the group of heroes works together to find the hidden locations of the Sinister Spellbooks and gain the knowledge necessary to banish the Wicked Wizard from the realm! Makes use of the 9 new spell cubes and the coop goals from Dropped Too Deep. Plays with 3 to 5 players!

description:
Endangered: TH2 + SiN is a micro-expansion for Endangered that consists of two Ambassadors based on the Tantrum House hosts and a "Strength in Numbers" bonus.

Originally available as an Add-on from the Kickstarter campaign for Endangered:  New Species.

Endangered: Center for Biological Diversity is a small expansion for Endangered that features new role powers for the Zoologist and the Environmental Lawyer.

Originally available as an Add-on from the Kickstarter campaign for Endangered:  New Species.

boardgame: Perry Rhodan Simulation: Die Raumflotte der Druuf
description:
Klappentext:
Thema des ersten Ergänzungsbandes ist die Raumflotte der Druuf. Ein von Insekten abstammendes Intelligenzvolk, unterwarfen sie  ihr eigenes Universum, bis sie durch ein Naturereignis in die Lage versetzt wurden, in die heimatliche Milchstraße einzudringen. An der Überlappungszone beider Universen kommt es zu den größten Raumschlachten seit dem Methankrieg. Eine Gefahr für die Galaxis: Zehntausende von Raumschiffen; ausgestattet mit modernster Technik - dem Lineartriebwerk - warten auf ihren Einsatz.
Auch dieser Band bietet wieder alles über Stärke und Zusammensetzung, Logistik und Organisation, Technik und Personal.

This expansion adds the faction "Druuf" to the game. This includes new units and background informations.

Klappentext:
Groß war der Schock im Jahre 1984, als die Ganymed mit Thora und Crest an Bord nach Hause ins Arkonsystem vorstieß. Die degenerierten Arkonidenherrscher waren abgesetzt und der Robotregent von Arkon war an ihre Stelle getreten. Vor tausenden von Jahren gebaut und programmiert, setzt er nun alles daran, an die große Zeit der Alt-Arkoniden anzuknüpfen. Die Raumflotte - lange vernachlässigt - wird wieder auf den neusten Stand gebracht und die Rüstungsindustrie wird wieder zum wichtigsten Industriezweig.
Neben den üblichen Hintergrundinformationen werden in diesem Ergänzungsband mehrere Hilfsvölker der Arkoniden vorgestellt (Zaliter, Ekhoniden und Zekloner).

This expansion including new units and background information for Arkons Robotflotte.

Best of Christmas is the newest member of the LOGO board game family, featuring all your favourite picture, theme and pot luck cards.

Covering all the things we love about Christmas it even has the cutest Christmas tree that you decorate as you play the game.

It's a perfect bite-sized festive flash of fun to get you in the Christmas spirit.

HOW TO PLAY: You are testing your Christmas knowledge to climb up and decorate your side of the Christmas Tree. You’ll be asked questions which you’ll have to answer correctly in order to dress up the tree. The first team to decorate all the way to the top puts up the gold star and wins the game.

boardgame: Perry Rhodan Simulation: Die Raumflotte der Terraner II
description:
Klappentext:
Mehr als 50 Jahre lang hatte Terra Zeit seine Raumflotte aufzubauen, immer mit der Angst im Rücken, daß eines Tages der Robotregent Terra entdeckt und in sein Imperium einverleiben will. Noch ist die Zahl der terranischen Großraumer sehr gering, aber die Anzahl an Kleinstraumern (Jäger und Zerstörer) und das bekanntermaßen exzellente Personal der terranischen Raumflotte können das zum Teil ausgleichen.
Dieser Ergänzungsband zeigt den Aufbau der terranischen Flotte von einigen zusammengeklauten Schiffen hin zu einer modernen und schlagkräftigen Flotte; abgerundet wird das Bild von den Werften auf dem Mond und den Verteidigungsanlagen auf den anderern Planeten des Sonnensystems.

This expansion adds new units and background to the terran faction.

Don't be left holding the Popcorn when it Pops! Popcorn Poppin' is a fast and fun game where players must pass the popcorn bucket before the popcorn pops. While the time counts down, players follow the instructions from the die and pass the popcorn as quickly as they can before the popcorn pops!
-Manufacturer's description

In  "Popcorn Poppin'", not to be confused with "Poppin' Popocorn", one player fills the plastic popcorn bucket with plastic popcorn pieces, start the time by pulling the cord on the back, and then press down the cardboard-and-plastic "die", which when released pops up and will roll onto a face showing what the player must do to pass the bucket of popcorn. Players keep doing this until the timer ends and the popcorn pieces are launched out from the bucket, making the player holding the bucket when this happens out for the round. It plays much like a game of Hot Potato with a jack-in-the-box from hell. The game continues until only one player is left. The last player left is the winner!

boardgame: Perry Rhodan Simulation: An der Überlappungsfront
description:
Klapptentext:
Die Auseinandersetzung zwischen den Druuf un den arkonidischen Robotraumern bilden den Hintergrund des ersten Szenariobandes. Hier werden die Gefechte und Auseinandersetzungen, die 17 Monate lang an der Überlappungszone zwischen den beiden Universenandauerten, aufgearbeitet. Neben einer Reihe von Szenarien wird in diesem Band auch auf den Verlauf der Kämpfe und die Verluste auf beiden Seiten eingegangen.

This expansion adds new scenarios to the game.

boardgame: Perry Rhodan Simulation: Die Raumflotte der Maahks I
description:
Klappentext:
Dieser Hintergrundband zur Perry Rhodan Serie stellt ausführlich die Raumflotte der Maahks zu Beginn des Methankrieges vor. Das Aufeinandertreffen zwischen den Arkoniden und den Methanatmern führte zu kriegerischen Auseinandersetzungen, die mehr als 300 Jahre dauern sollten. Auf Grund ihrer enormen Vermehrungsrate waren die Maahks in der Lage, ihre Verluste schnell auszugleichen - ganz im Gegensatz zu den Arkoniden, deren exzellente Besatzungen allmählich dezimiert wurden. Doch trotz ihrer zahlenmäßigen Überlegenheit mußten schließlich die Maahks das Schlachtfeld räumen. Ihre wenigen Überlebenden konnten sich durch den Sonnentransmitter retten und wurden von den Meister der Insel (Mdi) in der Zwergengalaxis Andro-Alpha angesiedelt.
In diesem Hintergrundband, der reichhaltig illustriert ist, werden folgenden Themen ausführlich behandelt:

Allgemeines. Wie kam es eigentlich zum Aufeinandertreffen zwischen Arkoniden und Methanatmern? Ausführungen über das Aussehen und Körperbau, Atmung und Körperchemie sowie ihrer Geschichte runden das Kapitel ab.

Aufgabenstellung. Nach dem ersten Gefecht mit den Arkoniden änderte sich auch die Aufgabenstellung in ihrer Flotte.

Stärke und Zusammensetzung. Jedes Jahr bekam der Imperator einen Tobsuchtsanfall, wenn die immensen Zahlen noch um einige Prozentpunkte nach oben kletterten.

Logistik. Werften Stützpunkte und andere Einrichtungen im Herrschaftsgebiet der Methanatmer werden vorgestellt. Nicht zu vergessen die Weltraumbahnhöfe zwischen den Galaxien.

Führung, Struktur und Organisation. Wie waren die Flotten aufgebaut, die sich mehrere Jarhunderte blutige Gefechte mit den Arkoniden liefern konnten.

Technik. Wie waren die Raumschiffe ausgerüstet, mit den die Maahks die Galaxies unsicher machten.

Personal. Selbst bei den Methanatmern gibt es einige Persönlichkeiten.

Datenblätter. Einzeln vorgestellt und mit Datenblättern versehen, werden die Raumer der Flotte aufgeführt.

Darüberhinaus bietet Ihnen dieser Band mehrere Szenarien für das Raumkampfsystem der Perry Rhodan-Simulation und für das Brettspiel "Der Methankrieg".

This expansion adds the race Maahks to the game. Including units, background information and a scenario.

Breukosaurus Rex is an education game that helps children to practise fractions and calculate percentages and decimals.

In the game, players try to get on the other side of the island. Each turn, the active player will roll the two dice and will afterwards see how much they get. The player choses either to step forward, take cards or do both.

On the cards they can either see fractions, calculate percentages or decimals and then they must see if they match several cards to make 1 whole or 100%. If they can do that they win an egg. To win the game a player must get to the other side with 3 eggs.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Schrödinger's Cat: A Game of Curiosity
description:
Schrödinger's Cat in a pocket-sized game of curiosity that unfolds in ten minutes and stretches beyond the natural lifespan of the players. Equal parts infinite and intimate, two players, or two teams, take on the role of either the Cat Wrangler or the Scuba Master, making permanent, irreversible, life or death choices for their cat. Their goal, of course, is to keep their cat alive as long as possible.
 Inspired by quantum physicist Dr. Edwin Schrödinger’s metaphysical thought experiment stating that a cat sealed in bunker with a container of radioactive poison - that may or may not have opened - is both alive and dead at the same time, this destructible game begs the question… does curiosity really kill the cat?

Perfect for curious players who revel in experiences that push the boundaries of what a game can be.

—description from the publisher

Rioolratten is an education game that helps children with reading comprehension.

To respond to the question he has to look threw the flyers, catalogue... If the player gives the right answer he needs to look on the back of the card how many steps he may go forward.

If the player doesn't give the right answer than he stays on is place. To win the game the player has to get to number 55.

—description from the publisher

"Blitzkrieg vs. Russia, 1941" *attempts* for the Barbarossa Campaign, to be what Frank Chadwick's classic little Battle for Moscow, 1st edition is for that battle:  a basic, relatively fast and simple, very conventional hex-and-counter introductory board wargame.

It is the same scale - 13 hexes from Warsaw to Moscow - as classic old SPI Barbarossa and might be used with that.  Stalingrad *is* on the South-Eastern edge of the board, thanks to canting.  And it fills to the maximum an A3/11x17 mapboard, although halves are provided for a larger, 17x21 one and larger pieces can be made from the single-set sheet.

Monthly turns, 37+2 German and Axis units, 30+5 Russian units.  Russian units hidden until adjacent/engaged with a couple blanks/dummies.

Blitzkrieg mechanics for the Germans, pressed attacks, factory evacuation and massive replacements for the Russians, on one of my more beautiful maps.

(I finally figured out how to make the background other than stark, glaring white.  Think fields of light green Russian grass with the darker green forest(, etc.).

This would have been good in the Four Roads to Moscow gameset.

—description from the designer

(TA)ALIENS, is an educational game that helps children with spelling, vocabulary and French.

In the game, players(the aliens) must get on a new planet because a comet is coming closer and endangering the planet. To be allowed to enter the new planet they need to collect 4 stars.

How to collect stars?
Players need to walk over the stars to reach the small planets.

In the pile of words are several cards stating the player can play another turn, the player has to move the comet forward or gets a chance to practice a word three times before writing it.

Mierzoet, is an educational game that helps children of all ages to learn more about healthy food, the food triangle and the nutrient code.

In the game, the player(the ants)needs to go to one of the piles, to collect 5 cards at the end of the game. Each turn, the active player will roll the die, and move his ant forward. When one of the players gets to the pile, he may pick a card. The goal is to select a card with as less sugars as possible. The player can take the card home and the ant flies back to the start circle in his own color.

Once each player collected 5 cards, they may turn the cards to compare which player has the healthiest cards. The player with the healthiest cards wins this round and gets a piece of fruit. The players can play as many rounds as they want. The ant colony which has the most pieces of fruit in the end wins the game.

Afterwards they can also see how healthy their food is with the food triangle or the nutrient code.

Enter into the world of Psychic Warfare.  To survive on this battlefield, you will need to see the future, read your opponent's mind while obscuring your own thoughts, move in for the kill when they least expect it, and, most importantly, survive by any means necessary.

ESPC is a fast-paced game where you try to be the last player standing.  A round should take about 5 minutes or less.  The last player left alive gets a victory point.  Cards in the game represent Psychic Powers. Each turn a player draws a card and declares what power they are going to use.  At this point, any player may challenge the use of this power.  If the player claiming to play the power doesn't have that card in hand, they are out for the round, but if the player has the card, the challenger is out.  At this point, the player uses their power card and discards face up.  This continues until only one player is left, and points are awarded.  Then the deck is reshuffled and the next round begins.  The game is played until one player reaches the target number of victory points to win.

Each time the game is played, only a subset of powers are used. The number of powers in a game is based on the number of players, and there are rules governing how powers are selected.  This leads to a unique experience each time the game is played. For a 4 player game there are 4,000+ different power combinations.

-description from designer

With Streifzüge durchs Salzburger Land (Forays through the regionof Salzburg), you get to discover or re-discover Salzburg, the heart of Austria, its districts and villages.
Get to know historical details, unknown sagas and stories about the state of Salzburg.

Tabik is a finite territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on an initially empty square board.

Both players must have shared access to a sufficient number of black stones, white stones and rods. Stones are square-shaped and placed on the spaces (squares) of the board. Rods are as long as stones are wide and placed on the edge segments between adjacent squares.

				
				
					DefinitionsIn these rules, "adjacent" always means "orthogonally adjacent".

A group is a stone along with all other stones one could reach from it through a series of steps between adjacent like-colored stones. Thus, all stones in a group are the same color.

The size of a group is the number of stones in it.

				
				
					PlayOn your turn, if possible, perform exactly one of these actions:

- Place two stones of different colors on adjacent empty squares and a rod on the edge segment between them.

- Swap the positions of two adjacent stones of different colors not separated by a rod and place a rod on the edge segment between them.

If you have no moves available, you must pass. Passing is otherwise not allowed.

The game ends when both players pass in succession. The player with the higher score in the final position wins. Your score is the biggest group size in which you have the greater number of groups. If there is no such size, your score is zero. If both players are tied at zero points, whoever made the last move loses.

				
				
					Pie ruleThe pie rule is used in order to make the game fair. This means White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with their opponent instead of making a regular move.

-description from designer

Fold & Fly! is a short family-style game for 2-50 players.

Each round, players fold lines on their airplane templates, then throw towards the finish line! Will your airplane make it past the finish line first? Or will a rival craft an even more aerodynamic machine? Or will planes collide - dashing hopes in the process!

Wythe is a finite territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (squares) of an initially empty square board. Both players must have access to a sufficient number of identical rectangular pieces. Seen from above, a piece has an area of 2x1 board squares. One of its two square halves is colored black, the other white.

				
				
					DefinitionsIn these rules, "adjacent" always means "orthogonally adjacent".

A bit is one of the two small one-colored square prisms that make up a piece.

A stack is a set of one or more bits piled onto each other on the same square. The color of a stack is the color of its topmost bit, which denotes its owner.

The height of a stack is the number of bits in it.

A group is a stack along with all other stacks one could reach from it through a series of steps between adjacent like-colored stacks. Thus, all stacks in a group are the same color.

The size of a group is the number of stacks in it.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, if possible, place a piece on the board so that it exactly covers two empty squares or two stacks that rise to the same height, without causing any bit to lie directly on a like-colored bit. At all times, at least one bit of every piece on the board must remain uncovered.

If you have no moves available, you must pass. Passing is otherwise not allowed.

The game ends when both players pass in succession. The player with the higher score in the final position wins. Your score is the biggest group size in which you have the greater number of groups. If there is no such size, your score is zero. If both players are tied at zero points, whoever made the last move loses.

				
				
					Pie ruleThe pie rule is used in order to make the game fair. This means White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with their opponent instead of making a regular move.

				
				
					NotesWythe is based on TAIJI.

-description from designer

Quarod is a finite territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (squares) of an initially empty square board.

Each player must have access to a sufficient number of square-shaped pieces of their own color. Each piece has a line (rod) connecting its center to the center of one of its edges.

				
				
					DefinitionsIn these rules, "adjacent" always means "orthogonally adjacent".

Two adjacent opposite-colored pieces are engaged to each other if their rods form a single continuous line.

A group is a piece along with all other pieces one could reach from it through a series of steps between adjacent like-colored pieces. Thus, all pieces in a group are the same color.

The size of a group is the number of pieces in it.

				
				
					PlayOn your turn, if possible, perform exactly one of these actions:

- Place two pieces of different colors on adjacent empty squares so that they are engaged to each other.

- Swap the positions of two adjacent pieces of different colors that are already engaged but not to each other, and rotate them so that they are engaged to each other.

If you have no moves available, you must pass. Passing is otherwise not allowed.

The game ends when both players pass in succession. The player with the higher score in the final position wins. Your score is the biggest group size in which you have the greater number of groups. If there is no such size, your score is zero. If both players are tied at zero points, whoever made the last move loses.

				
				
					Pie ruleThe pie rule is used in order to make the game fair. This means White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with their opponent instead of making a regular move.

-description from designer

BelloLudi Rifles rules have been developed for people who have no previous experience in wargaming, yet like to play games. 
BelloLudi designed these rules especially for team building purposes. It is therefore easy to learn, quick to play, yet difficult to master! Suitable for two to forty players!

The game is designed as a multiplayer game but will work just as fine with only two players or even solo. The same goes for the number of miniatures involved. It is possible to start with only 20-odd miniatures, but that can easily be expanded. Hopefully, playing the game will encourage you to create your own armies. Similarly, the distances are given in inches for use with 28mm scale soldiers. Feel free to adapt to centimetres should you not have the space or when you use a smaller scale of miniatures.

This ruleset simulates battles in the period of 1860 to roughly 1914, the age in which European countries forged empires all over the world and combated each other and the various indigenous people. The rules will work for skirmishes in colonial settings, the Franco-Prussian War and other Wars of German Unification, the Balkan Wars, the Spanish-American War, the Boer Wars, the Boxer Rebellion and other clashes. It will also work for the early clashes of WW1.

Designed for the beginner with a minimum of miniatures, it also allows you to field large armies on the table top and play to a conclusion in two to three hours.

The aim is not to annihilate the enemy unit to the last man, but to inflict so much damage that morale is brought down and the unit will become shaken or will run away in panic and be removed from the table top. By a clever combination of movement and shooting, dashing cavalry charges and carefully positioned artillery pieces, this can be achieved. The tactics are based on teamwork. Not only within a unit, but also between units and as such, the whole army.

This product come with two commander dice. Markers can be downloaded.
Cards are available separately.

—description from the publisher FB page

Treasure Island is a roll and move game first published during the 1920s in Australia. Players follow a path of 41 squares through the sea passing various ports along the way until you reach Treasure Island. There you roll specific numbers in order to collect treasure and acquire a ship to take you on a course of 31 squares for home.

Along the way contrary winds can detour your journey, you can drift off course, hit rocks and get wrecked, all of which make returning to home more difficult. First home is the winner.

At least 2 versions exist. A version resides in the Australian National Martime Museum, Sydney, with somewhat simpler graphics and classified as first edition

Players battle to the last gunfighter standing using dice, cards, poker chips, and miniature figures sculpted in the style of vintage cartoons. The game ends when only one character remains standing, or a miniature on horseback reaches the end of the time track. At the end of the game, the winner is determined by the total bounties of opponents eliminated, plus bonuses for found items and scenario objectives.

Heja Sverige is a strategic level 2-player wargame set in the latter half of the 30 Years War when Sweden re-ignited the war in Germany.

Then France got involved as well and it would take 18 more years for the hostilities to end, leaving a shattered Germany in its wake when the war finally ended in 1648.

One player is the Union Player and he'll control the Factions of Sweden, France and the Protestants.

The other player is the League Player and he'll control the Factions of Spain, Bavaria and the Holy Roman Empire (HRE)

The game is played for a maximum of 9 two-year turns on a map divided into Areas controlled by the various Factions and Neutrals.

Armies consisting of Infantry, Cavalry and Artillery combat units are moved around by Generals, engage in combat and take control of Areas looking to fulfil their objectives.

In Chai, you will step into the shoes of a tea merchant, combining tea flavors to make a perfect blend. Specializing in either rooibos, green, oolong, black or white tea, you will buy and collect ingredients to fulfill your customers’ orders.

As a tea merchant, each turn you will do one of the following: Visit the Market, Select Additives, Reserve a Customer.

At the end of each turn, a player may complete a tea order from one customer card in their hand or visible in the customer pool. A base tea token, tea flavors and additives shown on the card are needed ingredients, and placed in an empty tea cup. The player flips over a tip and receives a coin bonus, moving the thermometer round tracker up one notch if all cups are filled.

The game ends when five rounds of cups have been fulfilled. When the final order is completed, other players complete their last turn so that each player has played the same number of turns. The player with the most victory points (from customer orders, money, and awards) wins the game as best tea merchant!

In PixPlain, you invent creative stories about why your picture card, which is actually not suitable, goes particularly well with the word on display. For example, describe what a snail has to do with speed and why a lemon is cute. Select one of your picture cards that does not match the word at all, with which you want to challenge the other players. The selected cards are shuffled and dealt again. Now you have 30 seconds to argue why your assigned picture card does match the word. Vote on which story you liked best. The winner keeps his card. The first person to have 3 cards wins the game.

-description from publisher

You have found the entrance to a dungeon maze, but unfortunately you don't have a map. You know that you have to find the sword and defeat the dragon to get to its treasure. But be careful, your fellow player is trying to do the same in your dungeon.  Play with the pre-build dungeons or build your own. Decide in which direction you want to go on your map, if you go the wrong way or step into a trap, it's your opponent's turn. Whoever finds the sword and the dragon first wins the game.

—description from the publisher

You invite your friends for tea Time and want to enjoy an intimate conversation – while the other guests at the table are listening unintentionally. How do you manage to transmit the secret word without revealing yourself and the word category? Tealepathy is a word and association game. Choose a secret category with your changing team partner. Now it's up to you to convey a secret word within this category to him or her by giving clues. But be careful, the other players will try to find out which category it is. Depending on the vote of the audience, points are distributed to all players. Whoever has the most points after a round wins Tealepathy.

-description from publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
The INC Corporation has developed an artificial intelligence that  is indistinguishable from the humanoid. An international hacker  organization called HaXs 4 has hacked into the corporate network, aiming to destroy the AI. As the firewall of the INC Co. you are trying to create an impenetrable network over the coding so that the HaXs 4 have no chance to realize their plans. You have to work as a team to create a big firewall network. But everyone works on their own and just share little information. Roll the dice and establish fragments into your own Mind Map to build connections between the scripts to safe the coding of the AI. Every valid connection will score points to your team. The HaXs 4 get points by deleting connections. You win the game if you have more points as the HaXs at the end.

-description from publisher

Legends speak of ancient relics, locked away for untold centuries -- who knows what treasures and terrors await? Great power can be yours if you’ve got a villainous eye for looking past the other temptations of the vaults!

Relic Roadshow adds new temptations to Vault Wars allowing you to bid your way to the dark side! New Vaults are uncompromised by special rules and instead award the second-highest bidder with powerful Relics to craft! Drawn by the corrupting power, Aspiring Villains reward you for crafting their desired Relics. Vices give powerful, unique abilities... if you’re willing to take on a bit of corruption, of course! Step right up and place your bid!

Players will take turns as an Auction Master, leading a cut-throat bidding war to selling off Vaults full of items to the other players.
Each Vault provides a unique experience, introducing new ways for the players to bid.
No player quite knows everything that's in a Vault, and there's lots of junk to fool your opponents into bidding on.
Between auctions, players may sell off their items to gain Gold, or save them for Victory Points -- paying storage fees to keep them around.
Collect gems for massive Victory Points, Weapons to gain lots of Gold, or rare Artifacts to gain powerful abilities.
Each player has up-and-coming heroes who are looking for specific items, giving out bonus victory points (but try to keep that a secret from your opponents).
The player with the most victory points after all the auctions will be the winner!

An optional Kickstarter exclusive crossover character from the Middara board game which comes with the following that can be used with Neo-Morphosis Infestation:

1 detailed plastic Nyx miniature
1 ID card
3 x Ability cards

-description from publisher

Anachrony is a worker placement and tableau building game set in a foreboding future. As a leader of one of humanity's four ideological Paths, your goal is to prepare for an asteroid impact bound to happen over the course of the game, and become the dominant power on New Earth once the dust has settled. At your disposal are not only your loyal workers and high-tech Exosuits, but also the ability to open Time Rifts, implemented as an innovative game mechanism.

The Anachrony: Infinity Upgrade Pack Box includes every expansion and module released for the Anachrony: Fractures of Time KS and all stretch goals. This includes an exclusive box with plastic trays to hold all content provided and all previous content.

—description from the publisher

The Hot & Spicy expansion adds components for a 5th player, special player boards that give each player a special ability (and a place to store their chips, etc.), as well as some new spicy recipes that require chili! Make your dishes extra tasty, or try to cook one of the new recipes that require a sprinkling of spice!

The new Hot & Spicy recipe cards may be used instead of, or, shuffled in with, the recipe cards from the base game. Now chili is a required ingredient on the recipe cards! These recipes are earned the same way as regular recipes, but players now must spend 4 ingredient cards to learn them (3 ingredients, plus chili)

Components:
18 extra Ingredient cards
2 extra Chili cards
12 extra paper Yen
18 Hot & Spicy Recipe cards
7 yellow throwing chips
